Once inside, you'll be on a tour of the origins of chocolate and here in this impressive space you'll learn about chocolate's arrival in Europe, the way it was revered for both medicinal and nutritional properties, and get to sample the goods, too.

experiences.name: Costa Brava Boat Trips
experiences.description: Explore the beauty of Costa Brava on a romantic rose boat tour! Enjoy the stunning coastline, the sunshine, and the crystal-clear waters of the Mediterranean Sea. You and your loved one will have the perfect opportunity to create a lifetime of memories together. Relax and admire the incredible views and fresh air as you drift along the water. This boat tour is a must for anyone wanting to experience the beauty of Costa Brava!

experiences.name: Casa Amatller
experiences.description:
Casa Amatller is an eye-catching building designed in the Modernisme style by Josep Puig i Cadafalch. 
It features an array of fantastical busts and reliefs of dragons, knights and more on the outside. On the inside visitors can wander the rooms full of furniture from past resident, well-known businessman and chocolatier Antoni Amatller.

experiences.name: Castle of Cardona
experiences.description:
The Castle of Cardona was constructed in 886 and is considered one of the most important medieval castles in all of Spain. Sitting majestically on a hilltop, it offers eye-melting views of the surrounding valley and the Pyrenees. 
The building has undergone numerous renovations and extensions over the course of the centuries. You'll see elements of Romanesque and Gothic styles, plus the Minyona tower, where the young maid Adalés was allegedly kept as a recluse by her parents for being involved in an illicit affair!
Visitors can also explore the Church of Sant Vicenç, built in 1040 on the site of the old chapel of the castle. Don't miss the crypt below the presbytery – the Chapel of the Relics.

experiences.name: Tablao Flamenco Cordobes
experiences.description: Watch traditional flamenco performances by skilled dancers. Enjoy authentic Spanish cuisine while listening to live music.

experiences.name: Egyptian Museum of Barcelona
experiences.description: First opened in 1994, the Museo Egipcio de Barcelona (Egyptian Museum of Barcelona) is a museum of Egyptology located on Carrer de València, in Barcelona, Spain. It was the first museum of its kind in Spain, and exhibits over 1,100 artifacts from ancient Egypt in its permanent and temporary exhibitions.

experiences.name: Barcelona City Tours
experiences.description:
Explore Barcelona with a fun and exciting city tour! See the city's stunning architecture, lively culture, and beautiful scenery. 
Enjoy a guided tour of the city and get to know its vibrant neighborhoods. After meeting up with a friendly guide, they'll show you the best of Barcelona and help you make the most of your time in the city! 
Take your pick from a range of different tours that showcase the most interesting areas in Barcelona, and decide how you do it – by foot, by bus, by bike, or even by tuk-tuk!

experiences.name: Cardona Salt Mountain
experiences.description: One of the largest and oldest-such geological formations in the world, Cardona Salt Mountain is a cultural gem of Catalunya which was formed over two million years ago as dense rock pushed salt towards the Earth's surface. This mountain of salt was discovered by the ancient Romans and has been bolstering the local economy ever since. These days, you can plumb the cavernous salt mines with traditional guided tours and theatrical tours that cover the amazing history of the site.

experiences.name: RCD Espanyol Stadium
experiences.description: The RCDE Stadium is home to RCD Espanyol de Barcelona, a club with 120+ years of history. A tour of this spectacular stadium will give you a deeper insight into the ins and outs of the club, and take you through facilities including the press room, the first team dressing room, as well as a historical room featuring trophies and jerseys from all eras. Live out your football fantasies and pretend to be a legendary coach, player, or club president!

experiences.name: Codorníu Winery
experiences.description:
As soon as you arrive at the tall gates of Codorníu Winery, you'll get a sense of its heritage and tradition.
Since the 16th century, the family-run Codorníu business has been producing premium wines in Spain. From the late 20th century onwards, it became known for one of the world's best loved sparkling tipples: cava.
Through a guided tour of the winery, you'll learn the history of winemaking in the area, some of the secrets to cava's subtle flavors, and, of course, enjoy a tasting of their finest wares with an expert.

experiences.name: Barcelona Food Tours
experiences.description:
Explore the world-famous cuisine of Barcelona on a delicious food tour! 
You'll get an authentic taste of the city's culinary offerings from traditional tapas to local delicacies. Savor the flavors of Spain's second-largest city and learn about its history and culture from a friendly local guide.

experiences.name: Santa Maria del Mar Church
experiences.description:
Santa Maria del Mar is a stunning Gothic church located in the heart of Barcelona, Spain. Known for its impressive architecture and rich history, this church has become a must-see attraction for tourists and locals alike!
Construction on Santa Maria del Mar began in the 14th century, and the church was completed in just 55 years - an impressive feat for the time. Its stunning design features soaring vaulted ceilings and intricate stained glass windows, which provide a breathtaking display of light and color.
Over the centuries, Santa Maria del Mar has survived numerous disasters, including fires and earthquakes. Despite these challenges, the church has stood the test of time and remains a symbol of resilience and strength.
Visitors to Santa Maria del Mar can take a guided tour of the church, which provides a deeper understanding of its history and architecture. There are also regular masses and concerts held in the church, making it a vibrant hub of culture and spirituality.

experiences.name: Barcelona Water Sports
experiences.description:
Experience Barcelona on the water and book your spot on an exciting aquatic activity. 
Hop on a jet ski and ride some waves, or hold on tight as you and your friends bounce around on a banana boat. Wit the Mediterranean sea so close, don't miss your chance to make the most of it. 
With crystal blue waters and stunning views of the city, you'll have one of the most unique experiences in Barcelona. Whether it's your first time discovering water sports, or you're a seasoned pro – there's something for everyone.

experiences.name: Palau Baró de Quadras
experiences.description: The Palau Baró de Quadras is a private house that opens to the public for tours exclusively on Wednesdays. The building was designed by the Catalan Modernist architect Josep Puig i Cadafalch.

experiences.name: IKONO Barcelona (Arenas de Barcelona Shopping Centre)
experiences.description:
IKONO Barcelona is a immersive experience showcasing interactive art installations where you become the subject. 
Get lost in surreal and incredible scenarios and capture it all on camera at IKONO Barcelona.

experiences.name: Torre Bellesguard
experiences.description: The Torre Bellesguard is a lesser-known Gaudi attraction, hidden up in the hills of Barcelona. This castle-inspired neo-Gothic house later went on to serve as a foundation for some of Gaudi's work on the Sagrada Familia. Plus, visiting Torre Bellesguard is a great excuse to visit the Sarrià-Sant Gervasi area of the city.

experiences.name: Barcelona Museum of Nougat and Chocolate
experiences.description:
Museu de la Xocolata or the Museo del Chocolate in Spanish, takes you on a sweet journey through nougat and chocolate making in Agramunt. 
The museum shows how these treats became a big part of the local life. You'll get to see lots of old tools and machines, and even a replica of the Vicens family's 1933 workshop. It's a fun way to learn about a tradition, dating back to 1741, about how artisan nougat is made.

experiences.name: Barcelona Museum of Contemporary Art
experiences.description: The permanent collection at MACBA boasts around 5,000 works from the mid-20th century onwards.  There's enough mind-blowing art inside to keep you busy for hours. MACBA is located in is Plaça dels Angels, close to two Metro stations.

experiences.name: Columbus Monument
experiences.description: At the base of Barcelona's famous La Rambla stands this 60 m tall monument dedicated to Christopher Columbus (known in Catalan as Monument a Colom). Built in 1888 for Barcelona's Universal Exposition this monument was designed to let the world know that Christopher Columbus reported back to the Spanish Royal Family after he 'discovered' America.

experiences.name: Erotic Museum of Barcelona
experiences.description: The Erotic Museum of Barcelona is a sex museum located close to the Mercado de la Boquería in Barcelona, Spain. It offers visitors insight into erotica of the past through art and exhibits.

experiences.name: Barcelona Bike Tours
experiences.description:
Explore Barcelona on two wheels and experience the city like a local! 
These bike tours are the best way to get to know the city, with experienced guides to lead the way. Enjoy the sun and the breeze as you cycle past beautiful architecture, breathtaking views, and bustling markets. 
Get ready to discover hidden gems and popular hotspots as you cycle through the Catalan capital.

experiences.name: Freixenet Winery
experiences.description: Take a guided tour of the winemaking facilities. Sample different varieties of sparkling wine.

experiences.name: Barcelona Segway Tours
experiences.description:
Experience the thrill of gliding through the vibrant streets of Barcelona on a Segway tour! Get ready to explore this enchanting European city the easy way. 
Feel the wind in your hair as you swoop around the lively squares and admire the stunning architecture. Let your knowledgeable guide show you the best of Barcelona and its hidden gems.
Don't worry about learning to ride it, you'll have safety gear provided and plenty of instructions before you start the tour.

experiences.name: European Museum of Modern Art
experiences.description:
The European Museum of Modern Art (MEAM) is based in an old palace that was rebuilt in 1791 by a textile merchant in the La Ribera neighborhood, very close to the Picasso Museum in Barcelona.
When, in the mid-nineteenth century, Calle de la Princesa was opened to connect the old town with the Castell dels Tres Dragons in Ciutadella Park, the palace was divided into two parts, on both sides of the street.
The MEAM presents its own collections, mostly of figurative and realistic works, structured in three categories: contemporary art of the 21st century, modern sculpture of the 20th century, and Catalan sculpture of the 20th century, as well as other temporary exhibitions.

experiences.name: Big Fun Museum
experiences.description: The Big Fun Museum is an interactive exhibition located in Barcelona, Spain. Opened in 2018, it contains nine small museums dedicated to a range of colorful themes, including an upside down house and a museum of illusions.

experiences.name: Barcelona Helicopter Flights
experiences.description:
See Barcelona from a brand-new perspective with an exhilarating helicopter flight! 
Soar above the city, admiring stunning cityscape views and taking in the vibrant atmosphere from up high. 
Experience the best of Barcelona with a memorable journey that will stay with you for a lifetime! With comfortable seating and the latest safety features, you can sit back and relax as you enjoy the ride. Don't miss out on this truly unique opportunity.

experiences.name: ECLIPSO Barcelona
experiences.description: Explore a detailed recreation of the Titanic's interiors. Interact with exhibits that explain the ship's history and its tragic voyage.

experiences.name: Teatro Flamenco Barcelona
experiences.description: Watch live flamenco performances featuring skilled dancers and musicians. Enjoy an authentic cultural experience with traditional Spanish music and dance.

experiences.name: Barcelona Flamenco Shows
experiences.description:
Experience an evening of passion, grace, and fiery emotion with a flamenco show in Barcelona! 
Lose yourself in the captivating rhythms and vibrant energy of this traditional Spanish art form. Watch talented dancers move to the music, feel the energy of the passionate performance, and be amazed by the skill involved. 
Choose from a range of flamenco shows and enjoy the captivating beauty of flamenco during your stay in Barcelona!

experiences.name: Museum of Illusions Barcelona
experiences.description: Home to some of the quirkiest selfie backdrops in Barcelona, the Museum of Illusions is the perfect place to spend an hour of silly fun between tapas crawls and Barcelona's more highbrow cultural offerings. Who wouldn't want a selfie with the open jaws of a hungry T. rex?

experiences.name: Reial Cercle Artístic de Barcelona
experiences.description: View diverse art exhibitions and appreciate the works of local artists. Attend workshops and participate in creative activities.

experiences.name: Barcelona Bus Transfers
experiences.description: Take a comfortable ride to various destinations around the city. Enjoy convenient and timely service to reach key attractions and points of interest.

experiences.name: Car Racing Simulator
experiences.description:
Committed to expanding gamification offerings by entering fully into the world of motorsport and eSports with a new sim racing experience in Barcelona, with the quality guarantee of DigaliX, partners in eSports, gamification, and sim racing.
Experience IMSIM Alma simulators, equipped to replicate the physics and sensations of real racing. Test your skills and feel the G-forces, grip limits, weight transfer, engine revs, ABS, the loss of traction, and more.

experiences.name: Museu d'Història de Catalunya
experiences.description: The Museu d'Història de Catalunya is a museum of Catalonian history. It's located close to the port in an atmospheric building that also features an excellent rooftop terrace. It's easy to reach on foot and public transport from most parts of Barcelona.

experiences.name: Ice Bar Barcelona
experiences.description: The only place in Barcelona that maintains a bracing temperature of -5ºC all year round! Icebarcelona is the coolest spot in the Catalan capital and was designed by internationally renowned artists. Located right by Somorrostro beach, it makes for the perfect spot to chill away from the city's scorching sunshine during the hot months.

experiences.name: Tàpies Museum
experiences.description: The Tàpies Museum is an art gallery dedicated to modern, postmodern, and contemporary art. View a collection of works by Antoni Tàpies. Attend exhibitions and educational programs related to contemporary art.

experiences.name: Castell de Montjuïc
experiences.description:
In a comfortable small group and with your professional guide take the Montjuic funicular and get ready for the best views of Barcelona from top. Enjoy the breathtaking views from the Montjuic Cable Car and  wonderful views of Barcelona’s iconic skyline.
Reach the Montjuic Castle to enjoy a private visit allowed only to our small group. The Montjuic Castle has been a military fortress since its early beginning, witnessing the change of political power in the city. 
We invite you to an exclusive visit, not opened to general public, where you will be able to enter the dungeons of the several prisoners that have marked the history of the city.
Arise from the dungeons to the sky to admire a bird’s eye view of Barcelona and its surroundings. 
The best way to admire Barcelona in all its beauty is from high above the city. This is where Barcelona reveals itself in total splendour. With our exclusive visit, you will have access to the Tower , where you can admire a 360º views of Barcelona

experiences.name: Olympic and Sports Museum Joan Antoni Samaranch
experiences.description: Olympic and Sports Museum Joan Antoni Samaranch is located on Montjuïc, in Barcelona, Spain. The museum details the history of world sport and the Olympic Games, and contains a huge collection of sporting memorabilia.

experiences.name: Barcelona Synagogue
experiences.description: This Jewish religious building in Barcelona is one of the oldest in Europe. Learn about the life of the Jewish citizens of Barcelona before the Spanish Inquisition, their expulsion from the country, and their contributions to the culture of Catalonia.

experiences.name: Costa Brava Day Trip from Barcelona
experiences.description:
Discover the best of the Costa Brava on a day trip from Barcelona! Enjoy a full day of beautiful beaches, colorful villages, and stunning natural scenery. 
Explore the region's history, culture, and architecture or simply relax and take in the breathtaking views. With round-trip transport, you'll enjoy the ultimate day out without having to worry about the details. 
Don't miss your chance to experience the stunning beauty of the Costa Brava!

experiences.name: Casa Rocamora
experiences.description: The palazzo Casa Rocamora is the former home of collector Manuel Rocamora Vidal, who was born in Barcelona in 1892. The museum houses his collection of Alcora ceramics, paintings, drawings and sculptures from the 19th and 20th centuries as well as a trove of ancient clothing.

experiences.name: Andorra Day Trips from Barcelona
experiences.description:
Experience the magic of Andorra, amongst towering mountain peaks, with engaging day trips from Barcelona. 
These truly unique journeys offer a fabulous time out, providing a delightful blend of adventure and culture, all framed by Andorra’s exquisite alpine beauty. 
From breathtaking landscapes to vibrant local traditions, every enthusiastic traveler is destined to find something to love - a testament to the charm of this wonderful Pyrenean nation.

experiences.name: Antarctica Experience
experiences.description: See penguins in their natural habitat. Observe the ice formations and glaciers.

experiences.name: Barcelona Walking Tours
experiences.description:
There's some must-sees in Barcelona – the Sagrada Família, Park Güell, Casa Batlló – but aside from Gaudí's architectural gems, there's still so much of the city waiting to be discovered. 
A walking tour of Barcelona is the perfect way to discover the real Barcelona, thanks to the knowledge of the expert guides who accompany you. 
Discover areas like the Gothic Quarter, enjoy the taste of Catalonia with tapas, or get your fill of Picasso as you explore new areas of the city on a Barcelona walking tour.

experiences.name: Dalí Museum Day Trips from Barcelona
experiences.description:
Experience the thrill of a Dalí Museum day trip from Barcelona, a journey that fuses art, surrealism, and unparalleled scenic beauty. 
These day trips provide a riveting escape from the mundane, plunging you into the creative universe of one of the most famous surrealist artists, Salvador Dalí, without venturing far from the vibrant heart of Barcelona. 
Colourful, captivating, and culturally enriching, these escapes to the Dalí Museum promise a memorable splash of the avant-garde amidst the dazzling brilliance of Spanish heritage.

experiences.name: KBr Fundación MAPFRE - Barcelona Photo Center
experiences.description: KBr Fundación MAPFRE Barcelona is a hub for photographic art in the city. Found in the entertainment hub of Port Olímpic, the Barcelona Photo Center offers an ever-changing program of exhibitions highlighting masters of photography, contemporary photographers, and historical collections from Spain and Catalonia.

experiences.name: Simón Coll Chocolate Factory
experiences.description:
Simón Coll Chocolate Factory, or Espai Xocolata, is a chocolate factory in Barcelona. With a remarkable history that dates back as far as 1840, Simón Coll's full-functioning cocoa kingdom provides the best of old and new.
On a guided tour of Simón Coll Chocolate Factory you can check out a range of interactive exhibits, see chocolate being made by in-house chocolate wizards, admire antique machinery, and even try some of Simón Coll's melt-in-the-mouth chocolate.

experiences.name: Costa Brava Day Trips to Barcelona
experiences.description:
The sun-soaked shores of Costa Brava are all well and good, but sometimes you just need the inimitable charm and cultural riches of Barcelona. Get ready for a delightful blend of captivating history and lively culture that will make your heart sing. 
Arriving in Barcelona, prepare to be swept off your feet by the city's infectious energy and enchanting architecture. Stroll along Las Ramblas, a bustling promenade filled with street performers, colorful flower stalls, and mouthwatering food vendors. Indulge in some retail therapy at the trendy boutiques of Passeig de Gràcia or lose yourself in the Gothic Quarter's labyrinthine streets, where history whispers from every ancient stone.
Costa Brava day trips to Barcelona are the perfect blend of relaxation and excitement, where you can soak up the sun, immerse yourself in captivating history, and indulge in the vibrant spirit of Catalonia.

experiences.name: Old Estrella Damm Brewery
experiences.description: The Estrella Damm Old Brewery brings you the opportunity to discover how everything started more than 140 years ago. Through our tour, you will see the different spaces like the emblematic Kettle Room, the Power Generator Room or the Historical Archive; among others, that will introduce you on how the Damm beers were produced.

experiences.name: Vatican Museums
experiences.description:
A stay in Rome and Vatican City would simply be incomplete without a visit to the world-famous Vatican Museums. It’s one of the most visited museums in the world – upwards of 6 million visitors annually – and for good reason. 
Your Vatican Museum tickets allow you to access the full permanent collection – Michelangelo, Carvaggio, Raphael, and even Van Gogh and Matissee – as well as a wealth of other paintings, sculptures, tapestries, and historical artifacts from Egypt and Greece. 
While you’re there, don’t miss Michelangelo’s incredible ceiling frescoes located within the Sistine Chapel, or the gorgeous groomed gardens and courtyards between galleries. With so much to explore at the Vatican Museum, pick the right ticket to transform your experience from overwhelming to unforgettable.

experiences.name: Colosseum
experiences.description:
The Colosseum is a massive ancient amphitheater in the center of Rome. Picked apart by scavengers and ravaged by earthquakes and time, the Colosseum still stands as an impressive symbol of life in ancient Rome. It showcases the power of past emperors and the durability of the Eternal City.
This huge, marble and limestone structure was built to hold more than 50,000 spectators, all there to revel in the various forms of (mostly violent) entertainment, such as hunts, gladiator battles, and executions.

experiences.name: Rome Pantheon
experiences.description:
Once a pantheon to the gods of ancient Rome, this stunning structure in the heart of Italy's capital has been a church since the 7th century. 
Among its many spectacular features, is its perfect dome, designed and created in 120 AD, which is to this day the largest unreinforced concrete dome in the world.

experiences.name: Castel Sant'Angelo
experiences.description:
The towering cylinder of Castel Sant'Angelo, and its statue of Archangel Michael, is an instantly recognizable silhouette on the banks of the Tiber. 
Initially built as a mausoleum for Emperor Hadrian and his family, its purpose has changed many times over the years, from a fortress, a residence, a prison, and now a museum.

experiences.name: St. Peter's Basilica
experiences.description:
The Renaissance-era St Peter's Basilica is one of the largest churches in the world (and the home-church of the Pope). Highlights include the dome (the biggest in the world), Bernini's Baldacchino (the centerpiece of the church), and Michelangelo's Pietà (the only artwork he ever signed).
For both the pious and the casual visitor, a trip to St. Peter's is an awe-inspiring trip into the heart of Vatican City.

experiences.name: Rome Self-Guided Tours
experiences.description:
Discover the ancient wonders of Rome at your own pace with our self-guided tours! Our audio guides and mobile apps provide a seamless and immersive experience, allowing you to explore the city's iconic landmarks and hidden gems with ease. 
With flexible itineraries and expert commentary, our Rome self-guided tours offer the perfect blend of freedom and knowledge. Book now and embark on an unforgettable journey through the Eternal City!

experiences.name: Rome Hop-on Hop-off Tours
experiences.description:
There's no place like Rome. See the Eternal City on your own schedule with a hop-on hop-off tour. 
With frequent departures and arrivals, an informal narrated tour available in several languages, and convenient transport highlighting all the hotspots, it makes sightseeing stress-free. 
You can shuttle by the Colosseum, Circo Massimo, the Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill, among dozens of other stops along the route. 
You can even combine your Rome Hop-on Hop-off ticket with a travel card, and and see even more for your money.

experiences.name: Airport Transfer Rome (Fiumicino)
experiences.description: Catch a shuttle to central Rome for easy access to the city. Utilize the luggage services for convenient storage and handling.

experiences.name: Borghese Gallery
experiences.description:
Architect Flaminio Ponzio designed Villa Borghese for the cardinal and art collector Scipione Borghese, who wanted a party villa on the edge of town where he could house his enormous collection of priceless art. In 1901, the collection (and the gallery, and the park that surrounds it) was acquired by the Italian government, and opened to the public.
As a museum, Galleria Borghese punches well above its weight with an impressive hit rate of masterpieces. Sculptures by Bernini and Canova, paintings, by Caravaggio, Raphael and Titian... the list goes on.
The Borghese Gallery offers a unique museum visit with its strict capacity limit. You have only a 2-hour slot to explore, making each moment count. Borghese Galleria tickets are in high demand and sell out months ahead. It's crucial to pre-book your entry to ensure you don't miss out. Plan early and secure your spot to appreciate this exclusive opportunity.

experiences.name: Basilica of Santa Maria Maggiore
experiences.description:
The Basilica Papale di Santa Maria Maggiore is one of the oldest and most important shrines in the world, being one of a number dedicated to the Blessed Virgin Mary. 
This impressive Basilica is known in Italian as the Basilica Papale di Santa Maria Maggiore, and it remains the largest Roman Catholic Marian Church in Rome.

experiences.name: Barberini Palace
experiences.description: Barberini Palace is an important late-Baroque palace in Rome, packed with art and initially built for the Corsini family between 1730–1740. Guests can explore the different levels, see the library and browse the family's private art collections.

experiences.name: Capitoline Museums
experiences.description:
Consisting of four grand and historic palazzos, this complex of museums was designed by Michelangelo. Its large collection is devoted largely to the history of Rome itself, and it features many many artifacts that date back to antiquity. 
It's also considered to be the world's original museum. Pope Clementine XII opened it up to the Roman public in 1734.

experiences.name: Roman Forum
experiences.description: When Rome was Caput Mundi (Capital of the World), the Roman Forum was the very heart of the Roman Empire. Now a sprawling complex of ruins, it is here, a javelin's toss from the Colosseum, where everything happened: from making and enforcing laws to buying and selling exotic goods. Highlights include the Temple of Vesta, the Temple of Caesar, and the Senate House.

experiences.name: Catacombs of Saint Callixtus
experiences.description: The Catacombs of Saint Callixtus are some of the many smaller catacombs scattered around the Eternal City. Located on the Appian Way, the catacombs are famous for containing the Crypt of the Popes, which, as the name suggests, once contained the tombs of several popes from the 2nd to 4th centuries.

experiences.name: Olympic Stadium
experiences.description:
The Stadio Olimpico is located within the Foro Italico sports complex, north of the "Eternal City" of Rome.
It is home to both A.S. Roma and S.S. Lazio, and now visitors can take a tour, see into the dressing rooms, and even walk the same path that players walk onto the pitch.

experiences.name: Villa Medici
experiences.description:
Located on the scenic Pincio Hill, Villa Medici dates back to the mid-16th century and was designed by architect Giovanni Lippi. In 1576, the splendid villa was purchased by Cardinal Ferdinando de Medici to house his art collection. 
Historical guided tours offer visitors the chance to discover the Renaissance gardens of Villa Medici and the Cardinal's historic rooms.

experiences.name: Palatine Hill
experiences.description: Of Rome's seven hills, the Palatine is the one that's right in the heart of the ancient city. It overlooks the Roman Forum on one side and the Circus Maximus on the other. Some of the finest ancient homes were located here, including the emperor's – 'Palatine' is actually where the modern word 'palace' comes from!

experiences.name: Bioparco di Roma
experiences.description:
Bioparco di Roma is an open-air zoological garden and biopark located on the original Villa Borghese estate in Rome. 
Inside, you'll find 1,100+ animals of 222 species, spread across 17 hectares of gardens and enclosures.

experiences.name: Papal Palace & Gardens of Castel Gandolfo
experiences.description:
The Pontifical Villas, commissioned by Pope Urban VIII in the 17th century as a papal country home, are located 25 km south of Rome in the town of Castel Gandolfo. 
The property opened to the public in 2014 and covers 55 hectares, including the palace and large gardens built on the 2,000-year-old ruins of Emperor Domitian's residence.

experiences.name: Villa Borghese
experiences.description: Walk through the gardens and enjoy the sculptures. Visit the art gallery to see works by famous artists.

experiences.name: Mamertine Prison
experiences.description: The Mamertine Prison holds over 3000 years worth of treasures and is one of Rome's most interesting and important monuments from the Roman Republic. It's located below the Church of St. Joseph of the Carpenters and offers one of Rome's best views of the Ancient Forum.

experiences.name: Shuttle Bus to Rome Designer Outlet
experiences.description:
Explore the fashion epicentre of Rome in style, with a shuttle bus from the centre of the city to Castel Romano Designer Outlet! This shuttle bus will fuel your passion for fashion and take you to some of the best designer outlets in Rome, so you can shop and save on the latest styles. 
With comfortable seating and plenty of space for your purchases, this shuttle is the perfect way to get around Rome and find the best deals. Whether you're looking to stock up on designer deals or just window shop, this shuttle bus is the ultimate way to explore the city in comfort and style.

experiences.name: Vicus Caprarius - The Water City
experiences.description: Visit the ancient world at Vicus Caprarius - The Water City, located beneath modern Rome. Explore the fascinating subterranean ruins and aqueducts, and learn about the ingenious engineering of the past.

experiences.name: Airport Transfer Rome (Ciampino)
experiences.description:
The distance between Ciampino Airport and Rome's city center is just 12 km (8 mi), but traffic can make it tough to get there. The roads can become congested, and taxi fares add up fast.
Using the Ciampino Airport shuttle bus will save you the hassle of coordinating a rental car or private transport. You'll travel in comfort, whether it's one-way or round-trip. 
The Ciampino Airport shuttle bus is the most convenient way to travel stress-free – you'll have more time to see Rome and spend less time worrying about transportation.

experiences.name: Rome City Cards
experiences.description:
There's no place like Rome! Book one of our city cards and save time and money with combination tickets. 
Opt to visit a number of Roman attractions of your choice, plus unlimited public transport around the city for the duration of your city card. 
Spend more time in Rome (and less time in line) with skip-the-line entry to the Vatican Museums and the Sistine Chapel, plus entry to the Colosseum, the Roman Forum, Palatine Hill and more!

experiences.name: Rome Tiber Cruises
experiences.description:
Are you ready for an unforgettable adventure in Rome? Explore the Eternal City with a river cruise on the Tiber and take in some of the world's most iconic sights from a different perspective. 
From sunset cruises with an aperitif, to sushi-tasting experiences, to hop-on hop-off cruises, these sightseeing river cruises are sure to float your boat when in Rome.

experiences.name: Palazzo Colonna
experiences.description: The magnificent Palazzo Colonna has been home to over 20 generations of the Colonna family and contains one of the largest private collections of Baroque art in the world.

experiences.name: Capuchin Crypt
experiences.description:
The Navona Underground isn't the only piece of ancient history buried under the streets of Rome. 
Comprising five underground chapels located under Rome’s church of Santa Maria della Concezione dei Cappuccini, Capuchin Crypt is a haunting heritage site decorated with the bones of around 3,700 Capuchin friars! 
The macabre spectacle of skulls and bones was originally intended as a reminder of the ephemeral nature of life.

experiences.name: Palazzo Doria Pamphilj
experiences.description: On Rome's Via del Corso, the Palazzo Doria Pamphilj is one of the largest privately owned galleries in the city. It houses a significant collection of paintings, furniture and statues, and features regular live opera performances.

experiences.name: MagicLand
experiences.description: MagicLand is a family-friendly theme park with roller coasters, soft play areas, various live shows and performances, as well as a planetarium. It is located just 45 minutes outside of Rome.

experiences.name: Trevi Fountain
experiences.description:
The Trevi Fountain is one of Rome's most iconic landmarks, dating back to 1762. Located in the Trevi district of Rome, it stands at an impressive 26 m high and stretches 49 m wide. 
This iconic Baroque masterpiece beckons with its grandeur, crystal-clear waters, and the timeless tradition of tossing a coin for a return visit to the Eternal City.

experiences.name: Archaeological Park of Ostia Antica
experiences.description:
The Archaeological Park of Ostia Antica is an archaeological site based around the ruins of the ancient Roman city of Ostia, formerly a port and trading hub. It's known for the excellent preservation of its buildings, many of which contain stunning frescoes and mosaics. The history of the site dates back as early as the 4th century BC, while the oldest buildings on display – including the former military camp – date back to the 3rd century BC.
Ostia was the site of a furious battle during Sulla's civil war, sacked by pirates in 68 BC, and underwent renovations and improvements under Roman leaders including Julius Caesar, Tiberius, Claudius, and Trajan. It's a must-visit for history enthusiasts and anyone with an interest in Ancient Roman life.

experiences.name: Villa Gregoriana
experiences.description: Villa Gregoriana is a large park and nature reserve located in Tivoli, about 30 minutes from Rome, Italy. The park boasts several beautiful trails and Italy's second tallest waterfall, plus archeological ruins from different periods, including the once luxurious villa of the Roman Consul Manlius Vopiscus.

experiences.name: Rome Opera & Concerts
experiences.description:
Experience the magic of Rome with a classical opera or concert! From classical to modern to jazz, there's something for everyone in the Eternal City. Enjoy a night of music with friends and loved ones, and make memories that will last a lifetime. 
Whether you're looking for an intimate performance or a full orchestra show these concerts are sure to provide an unforgettable experience! Get your tickets now to experience the excitement of Rome's eternal music scene.

experiences.name: Terme di Caracalla
experiences.description:
Terme di Caracalla is one of the largest ancient thermal complexes in the world. When bathing here, Ancient Romans respected the order of cold room, warm room, and hot room. 
These magnificent baths of Emperor Caracalla were also a place for walking, studying, and relaxing.

experiences.name: Welcome to Rome
experiences.description: Located at the old Cinema Augustus on Corso Vittorio, Welcome to Rome is an interactive multimedia exhibition that introduces visitors to the epic history and architectural masterpieces of the Eternal City.

experiences.name: Leonardo da Vinci Museum
experiences.description:
Located in the basement of the Basilica of Santa Maria del Popolo, the Leonardo da Vinci Museum sits in the beautiful Piazza del Popolo, just a quick walk from Rome's Spanish Steps and Villa Borghese. 
Your ticket for the Leonardo da Vinci Museum is a window in the mind of a genius who left a lasting legacy on the world. Wander themed areas based on Leonardo's studies and passions. Discover interactive machines made by Italian craftsmen, based on Leonardo's manuscripts. See studies of his most famous Renaissance works. Pore over sketches of human anatomy, and watch in-depth videos on The Last Supper and The Vitruvian Man. There's also an immersive multimedia room that will take you on a journey into the life and mind of Leonardo da Vinci!

experiences.name: Catacombs of Domitilla
experiences.description:
The Catacombs of Domitilla are not the most visited in Rome, but remain some of the most important. Some of the rooms are richly decorated, others house the tombs of the martyrs who, during the Middle Ages, attracted the devotion of numerous pilgrims.
This sprawling underground Christian cemetery is named after the Domitilla family, who ordered them to be dug. The Catacombs of Domitilla are situated over 16 meters underground, about two kilometers from the south of Appia Antica, and span 15 kilometers in distance beneath the streets of Rome.

experiences.name: Leonardo da Vinci Experience
experiences.description: The Leonardo da Vinci Experience is located on Via della Conciliazione, one of the most historic roads in Rome. The 500-meter-long street links Castel Sant'Angelo and Saint Peter's Square and is packed with monuments, including the Palazzo Della Rovere and the church of Santa Maria in Traspontina.

experiences.name: Ara Pacis Museum
experiences.description:
No trip to Rome is complete without a visit to the glorious Ara Pacis Museum, where the ancient and contemporary worlds collide.
The museum showcases the Emperor Augustus’s amazing ‘Altar of Peace’ wrapped inside a magnificent travertine and glass blanket designed by contemporary architectural maestro Richard Meier.

experiences.name: Catacombs of Priscilla
experiences.description: Explore ancient burial sites and see early Christian frescoes. Take a guided tour to learn about the historical significance of the catacombs.

experiences.name: Mostra di Leonardo
experiences.description: This fascinating exhibition is dedicated to the timeless genius that was Leonardo da Vinci. Featuring reproductions of some of his most forward-thinking inventions, and a newly animated hologram section, the Mostra di Leonardo puts you right into the heart of the Renaissance. This exhibition is located in the center of Rome, around the corner from Campo di Fiori and within walking distance of many of the Eternal City's best attractions.

experiences.name: Rome Gladiator Experience
experiences.description:
Take a step back in time and experience the glory of the Roman Empire with a gladiator experience in Rome. This thrilling and unique activity gives you the chance to get immersed in Rome's ancient gladiator death matches, dressed in authentic gladiator gear!
Surround yourself in the sights and sounds of Ancient Rome and feel the energy of the crowd as you imagine what it was like to be a part of this legendary spectacle of hand-to-hand combat. Enjoy a truly unforgettable experience in the Eternal City!

experiences.name: Rome City Tours
experiences.description:
Rome may not have been built in a day, but you can sure see most of it in a short amount of time with a city tour of Rome.
From spooky night tours laden with history, to guided sunset strolls, or cruising around on a bike or a scooter tour, Rome is a sightseer’s paradise. 
Join other food-lovers on a tasting tour, and then walk off the pasta and pizza with a  walking tour – there's something for all travelers on a Roman holiday.

experiences.name: Altar of the Fatherland
experiences.description: Rome's Altare della Patria is located in Piazza Venezia. Also known as the Victor Emmanuel II Monument, construction started at the end of the 19th century, and this giant marble structure was completed in 1925. The name Altare della Patria translates as “Altar of the Fatherland”, and it's a popular meeting point in Rome. Visitors can climb to the top for stellar views of the city.

experiences.name: Pompeii Day Trips from Rome
experiences.description:
See more on your trip to Italy with an exciting day trip to Pompeii from Rome! Take advantage of this opportunity and explore the archeological sites and preserved ruins of the once-thriving city.
Observe how the city of Pompeii used to look like in ancient times and see remnant structures of streets, houses, and neighborhoods that have been discovered and protected. 
Escape the touristic crowds of Rome and visit this historic city on a quick and easy day trip.

experiences.name: Case Romane del Celio
experiences.description: Rome's Case Romane del Celio is an underground site that hides 20 rooms dating from between the second century and fourth century A.D. It is located underneath the Basilica Santi Giovanni e Paolo in Rome, Italy.

experiences.name: Rome Cooking Classes
experiences.description:
Discover the secrets of Italian cuisine with a cooking class in Rome! Immerse yourself in the city's culture and learn how to prepare simple but delicious dishes with the help of a local chef. 
You'll be able to watch, listen and ask questions, and maybe even sample some of the delicious recipes you've made! With a cooking class in Rome, you'll be able to take the tastes of Italy home with you.

experiences.name: Vatican Gardens
experiences.description:
The Vatican Gardens are private urban gardens in the Vatican, covering an area of approximately 23 hectares (more than half of the country!). Established during the Renaissance and Baroque era, the gardens are decorated with typically ornate fountains and sculptures from these times.
Visiting the gardens requires booking a private tour, as no general public access is allowed. Additionally, if you'd like to visit, always book in advance as tickets are usually sold out.

experiences.name: Rome Bike rentals
experiences.description:
Discover the Eternal City by bike! With the bike rentals available in Rome, you can explore the city's winding streets and picturesque alleyways in a fun, energizing way. Take in the sights, sounds, and smells of this historic city as you pedal your way through its historic streets. 
Whether you're looking to take a leisurely ride or a more challenging one, there's something for everyone. Enjoy the freedom of two wheels and make the most of your stay in Rome!

experiences.name: Rome Luggage Storage
experiences.description:
If you're planning a trip to Rome, don't let your luggage weigh you down! With this convenient luggage storage service, you can easily stow your bags in a safe and secure location while you explore all that the city has to offer. 
Enjoy the sights, sounds, and flavors of Rome without having to worry about where to leave your bags. This top-notch service makes it easier than ever to make the most of your vacation. Get ready to make lasting memories of your time in Rome!

experiences.name: Basilica di San Giovanni in Laterano
experiences.description:
St. John Lateran, the Pope’s cathedral, is the oldest basilica in Christendom, built in the 300s and restored in the 1700s. 
It’s home to the Holy Door, opened by the Pope once every 25 years. Nearby are the Sacred Steps, believed to be the steps Jesus walked during the Passion and brought here from Jerusalem.

experiences.name: Wax Museum Rome
experiences.description: The Wax Museum Rome displays wax statues of celebrities and historical figures. It is located behind the Piazza Venezia, ten minutes' walk from the Pantheon.

experiences.name: Domus Aurea
experiences.description: The Domus Aurea was a huge landscaped palace built after the great fire in 64 AD by Emperor Nero. It's located in the heart of Rome and is open to the public for guided tours every Saturday and Sunday.

experiences.name: Cinecittà World
experiences.description: This $338 million family-friendly theme park celebrates the triumphs of Italian cinema. Built by, and dedicated to, the original Cinecittá, this theme park is located on the edge of Rome, about 25km from the city.

experiences.name: Public Transport Rome
experiences.description: In Rome, you can use an extensive public transport network that includes buses, trams, and metro lines. The metro has three lines that cover major tourist spots and key areas in the city. Buses and trams provide additional routes to help you reach your destination.

experiences.name: Museum of Illusions Rome
experiences.description: Psychology, mathematics, science and biology come together in a mind-bending medley of attractions at the Museum of Illusions Rome.  It's all sleight of hand at this museum located on Palazzo Spadain the heart of Rome, and nothing is what it seems!

experiences.name: Rome Photoshoots
experiences.description: Capture memories with professional photographers at famous landmarks. Create personalized photo albums to cherish forever.

experiences.name: Aquafelix
experiences.description: Aquafelix is one of the top water parks in Italy. Opened in 1995, the park is located close to the port town of Civitavecchia and is popular with visitors disembarking from cruise ships, as well as those journeying from Rome. The Italian capital is just over an hour's drive away.

experiences.name: Christmas World at Villa Borghese
experiences.description: Enjoy festive decorations, themed exhibits, and exciting rides. Visit food stalls and try holiday treats.

experiences.name: Palazzo Altemps
experiences.description:
Just between Piazza Navona and the Tiber River, Palazzo Altemps is a beautiful late-15th-century palazzo. Now one of the branches of the National Roman Museum, it was originally built for Girolamo Riario, a nephew of Pope Sixtus IV (who was a keen practitioner of nepotism). 
The building changed hands a number of times throughout the centuries, before being gifted to the Italian State in 1982. It then underwent 15 years of restoration work, and was inaugurated as a museum in 1997.

experiences.name: Cinecittà Studios
experiences.description: This enormous film studio in Rome is a hotbed of Italian cinema, and has been the location for productions from cinema titans like Federico Fellini, Sergio Leone, Francis Ford Coppola and Martin Scorsese.

experiences.name: Church of St. Ignatius of Loyola
experiences.description: See intricate Baroque architecture and detailed frescoes. Don't miss the main fresco, which uses an optical illusion to make the ceiling appear open to the sky and the tombs of saints such as St. Aloysius Gonzaga, St. Robert Bellarmine, and St. John Berchmans.

experiences.name: National Etruscan Museum of Villa Giulia
experiences.description: The National Etruscan Museum is a magnificent Renaissance palace built for Pope Julius III between 1550 and 1555. Surrounded by beautiful gardens, the museum collection is devoted to the Etruscan civilization. The terracotta funerary monuments and the sarcophagi from the 5th century BC are among the highlights of the collection. Located on the north side of the Villa Borghese, Villa Giulia is stunning inside and out.

experiences.name: The Stadium of Domitian
experiences.description:
The Stadium of Domitian was commissioned by Emperor Titus Flavius Domitianus in 80 AD (the same year he also completed the Colosseum). The stadium is located to the north of Rome's Campus Martius and it was Rome's first permanent venue for athletic sports. In its heyday, it could hold up to 20,000 spectators.
This impressive UNESCO site is linked to Piazza Navona, which was built 4.5 meters above the ruins. Entry to the underground stadium has only been open to the public since 2014.

experiences.name: Amalfi Coast Day Trips from Rome
experiences.description: Feast your eyes on the hypnotic hues of the pristine Mediterranean Sea as you whirl away on an unforgettable day trip from Rome to the sparkling Amalfi Coast! Feel the sheer exhilaration of exploring quaint cliffside villages, indulge in delectable local cuisine, and soaki up the sun on golden beaches. Bask in this slice of Italian paradise, where every twist and turn offers new vistas of breathtaking beauty and allure.

experiences.name: QC Termeroma
experiences.description: QC Termeroma is a luxury health spa and leisure center just outside of Rome, Italy. It's famous for its thermal spa and luxurious massage treatments, and is inspired by the bathhouses of ancient Rome.

experiences.name: Palazzo Venezia
experiences.description: The Palazzo Venezia has served many purposes over the years, functioning as a papal palace, a political base, and, more recently, a museum focused on applied arts. The Palazzo Venezia museum has an enormous collection spanning around 800 years, including prints, ceramics, furniture, paintings, sculptures, and much more.

experiences.name: Rome Food Tours
experiences.description:
It's no surprise the Italian capital is teeming with mouth-watering food and beverage experiences that are just as essential to the Eternal City's endless menu of cultural offerings as the Colosseum or the Pantheon. 
From cooking classes to epic street food tours to luxury gourmet dinners with wine, these culinary experiences will help fuel your sightseeing adventures in Rome, if they don't outshine them!

experiences.name: Rome Walking Tours
experiences.description:
Explore the ancient wonder of Rome with one of the Eternal City's many exciting walking tours! See the city's most iconic landmarks, soak up the culture, and hear stories about Rome's fascinating history. 
Knowledgeable guides will show you the hidden gems of this incredible city and make sure you get the most out of your visit. Enjoy the freedom of he city and make sure you don't miss a single thing in this incredible destination.

experiences.name: Villa d’Este
experiences.description: Villa d'Este is one of the most magnificent villas in Italy. The Renaissance-era villa's gardens are resplendent with the sound of water features and birdsong, and make for a great retreat on hot and sunny days!

experiences.name: Archeological Area Largo Argentina
experiences.description: The Archeological Area Largo Argentina is a historic square in Rome, Italy, known for its four Roman temples and the remains of Pompey's Theatre. Historians believe Julius Caesar was assassinated here at the Curia of Pompey. The area also serves as a sanctuary for a colony of stray cats!

experiences.name: Trajan's Market
experiences.description:
The Markets of Trajan give a tantalizing glimpse of what Rome was really like at the absolute height of its power, under the Emperor Trajan.
This ancient landmark was a high-end shopping mall complex, designed by one of the greatest architects of ancient Roman times. It now houses a museum dedicated to the various extensions to the Roman Forum that were built from Julius Caesar’s time onwards.
The site is fully wheelchair accessible and open seven days a week.

experiences.name: Zoomarine
experiences.description: Zoomarine is a watery playground for aquatic animals and people. This amusement park/aquarium is located just outside of Rome, on the Italian coast. With its many shows and attractions, it's one of the best things to do during the hot summer months. In fact, it's frequently voted one of the best amusement parks for families. There are dolphin and sea lion shows, a sea turtle rescue and rehabilitation center, and waterslides, rollercoasters, and more.

experiences.name: Tivoli Day Trips from Rome
experiences.description: Escape the bustle of Rome for a day and explore Tivoli, a hidden gem nestled in Italy's stunning landscapes. From breathtaking architectural wonders to divine natural formations, Tivoli promises a feast for the eyes and a soothing respite for the soul. Get ready to be awe-struck by the rich historical lore and the artistry that has stood the test of time in this quaint Italian town!

experiences.name: Rome Catacombs and Underground Tours
experiences.description:
If you thought Rome was impressive, wait until you see what lies under the city.
Ancient crypts, catacombs, and necropoles have been buried under the Italian capital for thousands of years, just waiting to be discovered. 
Opt for a guided tour to uncover the secrets of ancient burial grounds, or explore the papal tombs within the walls of St. Peter's.

experiences.name: Catacombs of San Sebastiano
experiences.description:
The site of the Catacomb of St. Sebastian was once a deep depression used as a quarry for pozzolan, known as Ad Catacumbas (meaning "near the cavities"). This term has become synonymous with the underground cemetery. 
Since the first century, the site has been extensively exploited and built upon. The underground tunnels were repurposed for burial niches, while several columbaria were constructed on the surface. Emperor Constantine (306-337) built a basilica in the shape of a Roman circus.

experiences.name: Lateran Palace
experiences.description: Located on St. John's Square in Lateran on the Caelian Hill, the Lateran Palace is an ancient palace of the Roman Empire which was later converted into the main papal residence in southeast Rome. Today, the palace is now used by the Vatican Historical Museum, which illustrates the history of the Papal States.

experiences.name: Day Trips from Rome
experiences.description:
Rome is a great starting point for exploring other must-see Italian cities.  
Take a day trip north to Venice for its famous gondolas, or head south to visit the ancient ruins of Pompeii. Explore the Amalfi Coast or the island of Capri for a change of scenery.
These trips offer a chance to see more of Italy beyond the Eternal City.

experiences.name: Titanic - A Voyage Through Time
experiences.description: Learn about the history of the Titanic through detailed exhibits. Explore the ship using virtual reality, both as it exists today and as it was during its voyage.

experiences.name: Palazzo Braschi
experiences.description:
Situated in the heart of historic Rome overlooking the exquisite Piazza Navona, Palazzo Braschi is a real treat for lovers of history and art. This neoclassical delight, originally built in the late 18th century for the nephew of Pope Pius VI, is now owned by the City of Rome and houses the hugely impressive Museum of Rome. 
The collection comprises more than 100,000 works of arts and crafts and is the most important of its kind in the Eternal City. This museum is wheelchair accessible.

experiences.name: Luneur Park
experiences.description: Ride the Ferris wheel for a view of the city. Enjoy the many amusement park attractions and games.

experiences.name: Roma World
experiences.description:
A day at Roma World transports you back to a thriving Roman empire. Out in the serene Italian countryside, you'll spend the day eating, farming, shopping, and interacting with nature, just like they did in ancient times. 
Learn to wield a sword like a true Roman legionary, and watch fascinating gladiator and birds of prey demonstrations. Participate in educational workshops about animals and biology, discover the principles and flavors of traditional Roman food, and so much more, at this magical, historical theme park.

experiences.name: Basilica of St. Agnes Outside the Walls
experiences.description:
The Basilica of St. Agnes Outside the Walls, or Sant'Agnese fuori le Mura, is a huge and "perfectly-ruined" 4th-century basilica located behind the church of Santa Costanza in Rome, Italy.
The Catacombs of St.Agnes, located on-site, date from the middle of the 3rd century and provide an incredible insight into the Christian history of the Eternal City. For the best experience, join a guided tour!

experiences.name: Parco Archeologico Appia Antica
experiences.description:
There are a variety of exciting ways for you to experience the historical wonder, Parco Archeologico Appia Antica! Learn about the rich history, archaeological treasures, and captivating stories that make this site come alive on a guided walking tour.
Pedal your way through history on a thrilling bike tour! Or take control of your exploration with a self-guided audio tour. However you choose to enjoy it, the serenity of the surroundings cannot be missed.

experiences.name: Tuscany Day Trips from Rome
experiences.description:
Escape the crowds of Rome and journey to the Italian countryside to relax and unwind in the region of Tuscany. 
Sip some local wines, indulge in fresh pasta dishes, and explore the area known for its significance in the Renaissance era. Visit iconic landmarks of the region and get the chance to admire the art and architecture famous in Tuscany.

experiences.name: Florence Day Trips from Rome
experiences.description: Escape from Rome's hustle to the enchanting city of Florence for a day! With its vibrant culture, breathtaking scenery, and captivating Renaissance art, every moment spent in Florence unfolds like a well-written novel. From strolling along its cobblestone streets to indulging in mouthwatering Tuscan cuisine, these day trips offer an unforgettable journey through the soul-stirring beauty of Florence!

experiences.name: Villa Adriana
experiences.description:
Villa Adrian is the beautiful ruins of Emperor Hadrian’s lavish retreat. It has pools, steam baths, fountains and gardens (and more).
The Villa Adriana at Tivoli combines elements of the architectural heritage of Egypt, Greece and Rome into the form of an 'ideal city'. Originally intended as a retreat, Hadrian made it his official residence around 128 AD, where he lived with his court, governing Rome from just outside the city.

experiences.name: The Catacombs of Saints Marcellinus and Peter
experiences.description: The catacomb of Peter and Marcellinus is a major attraction in Rome, located on the ancient Via Labicana, south-east of the city and 3 kilometres from the ancient town's walls. You can join a guide to explore a vast network of subterranean galleries, 4.5 kms long, spanning three levels across an area of three hectares. This complex catacomb is the final resting place of saints Peter and Marcellinus as well as up to 25,000 more people. It's just one of the 60 Christian cemeteries in Rome, yet it's home to roughly a third of all the paintings known to exist in the catacombs.

experiences.name: Rome Fiumicino Airport Lounge
experiences.description:
Discover a world of comfort and convenience at Fiumicino Airport Lounge in Rome. With cozy seating, complimentary snacks and drinks, and free WiFi, this is the ideal spot to rest and relax before or after your travels. 
You can also take advantage of the range of services and amenities, such as luggage storage, duty-free shopping, and currency exchange. Enjoy a hassle-free experience with all the comforts of home!

experiences.name: Chiesa di Santa Maria Immacolata
experiences.description: aka Chiesa della Beata Vergine Immacolata

experiences.name: Palazzo Valentini
experiences.description: The Palazzo Valentini was built by Cardinal Michele Bonelli in 1585. Since 1873 it has served as the base of the provincial and prefectural administration of Rome. Recent excavations have uncovered archaeological remains of Ancient Rome in the palace's basement!

experiences.name: Hard Rock Cafe Rome
experiences.description:
Dine like a Roman emperor on a tasty break from Rome’s ancient attractions! It's a modern-day historical experience at the Hard Rock Cafe Rome, located with eye-popping views of the city in action.
After your classic American meal, make sure you check out the exclusive Hard Rock Cafe Rome merchandise in the gift shop.

experiences.name: Orvieto Day Trips from Rome
experiences.description: Venture on a delightful journey from the bustling heart of Rome to the serene beauty of Orvieto! This day trip captures the soul, offering a scenic escape filled with historic wonders, charming streets, and delicious local cuisine. Come experience a remarkable getaway, where the classic allure of Italy shines in every little cobblestone street and every warm smile you encounter!

experiences.name: Aqua World Rome
experiences.description:
Explore the aquatic wonders at Aqua World Rome, where entertainment and education come together. Witness exhibits featuring a variety of marine life, from colorful tropical fish to impressive sea creatures.
With interactive displays and engaging presentations, Aqua World Rome offers a unique and memorable experience for visitors of all ages. It's a must-visit for anyone keen on knowing the wonders of the underwater world.

experiences.name: AS Roma Football Games
experiences.description: AS Roma Football Games are a significant part of Italian sports culture. Fans from around the world tune in to watch their favorite team compete in high-stakes matches. If you're a sports enthusiast, these games offer a chance to see top-tier athletes in action. With strategic plays and an electric atmosphere, watching an AS Roma game is an exciting way to engage with the world of football.

experiences.name: Teatro Marcello
experiences.description: Located in the heart of the city, Teatro Marcello is a captivating architectural marvel that dates back to the Roman Republic era. As you approach the theater, you'll be in awe of its grand façade, adorned with elegant columns and intricate details. Step inside, and you'll be transported back in time, imagining the vibrant performances that once graced its stage. Teatro Marcello was a significant venue for theatrical productions and public spectacles during ancient Roman times. Today, the theater serves as a reminder of Rome's glorious past and hosts various cultural events and performances. Take a moment to appreciate the sheer magnificence of the structure, marvel at its well-preserved remains, and imagine the echoes of ancient applause that once filled the air. Teatro Marcello is a captivating destination that allows you to immerse yourself in the rich history and artistic legacy of Rome.

experiences.name: Piazza del Popolo
experiences.description: Located at the northern entrance of the city, Piazza del Popolo has been a gathering place for centuries, drawing locals and visitors alike. As you step into the square, you'll be captivated by its grandeur and architectural splendor. The centerpiece of the square is the iconic Egyptian Obelisk, flanked by twin churches, Santa Maria del Popolo and Santa Maria in Montesanto. The square is surrounded by elegant buildings and cafes, offering a perfect spot to relax and soak in the bustling ambiance. From Piazza del Popolo, you can embark on a journey to explore the city's iconic landmarks. The square serves as a gateway to the vast expanse of Villa Borghese, a lush park offering a peaceful retreat from the urban bustle. Piazza del Popolo is also within close proximity to the Spanish Steps and the renowned designer shops along Via del Corso. Immerse yourself in the lively energy and historical significance of Piazza del Popolo, where art, history, and the essence of Rome converge.

experiences.name: Piazza Navona
experiences.description: Piazza Navona is a public square in central Rome that has served as an important commercial and cultural hub for centuries. Awash with elegant fountains, ornate sculptures, street performers, and bustling crowds, it's located a stone's throw from the Pantheon, the Stadium of Domitian, as well as lots of restaurants, bars, and café terraces.

experiences.name: Seine River Cruises
experiences.description:
When in Paris, a cruise on the Seine is a must. But how to choose from the many Seine river cruises on offer? Whether you're looking for a simple sightseeing cruise or something a bit more classy, there are plenty of choices available. 
If it's relaxation and education you want, some Seine cruises come with an included audio guide so that you can take in the city with an expert. Elsewhere there are aperitif, lunch, and gourmet dinner options, so you can cruise around the city to your heart's (and stomach's) content.
You can find discounts, deals, inclusions, and more info about Seine boat tours on this page. All that's left is to hop aboard and explore!

experiences.name: Palace of Versailles
experiences.description:
This luxurious 17th-century French palace is a masterclass in excess. It has hundreds of acres of magnificently landscaped gardens, more than 2,300 rooms, a large collection of French Renaissance art, an opera house, and a 75-meter-long hall of mirrors lined with priceless statues and busts. There are no half-measures in its conception or execution – it even has more than 1,200 fireplaces!
It's one of the most visited attractions in France, as well as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Take your pick of Palace of Versailles tickets and tours – you won't regret it!

experiences.name: Musée d'Art Moderne de Paris
experiences.description: See roughly 15,000 works of modern and contemporary art spanning the 20th and 21st centuries. Attend special exhibitions and cultural events.

experiences.name: Musée d'Orsay
experiences.description:
Housed in the stunning Beaux-Arts style Gare d'Orsay railway station, the Musée d'Orsay is home to significant and stunning works by painters including Gauguin, Monet, Manet, Degas, Renoir, Cézanne, and Van Gogh.
It's widely considered to be one of the most culturally important museums in the world and hosts the most significant Impressionist and Post-Impressionist works ever created.

experiences.name: Fondation Louis Vuitton
experiences.description: Opened in October 2014, the Frank Gehry-designed Fondation Louis Vuitton is a stunning building dedicated to art, culture, and heritage. It can be found in the Jardin d'Acclimatation in Paris's 16th arrondissement.

experiences.name: Disneyland® Paris
experiences.description:
When you’re this close to the magic, why not? Add one Disney day to your Paris stay!
Disney® Parks are full of activities for all ages, so get ready to escape to your favorite worlds from Disney, Pixar, Marvel, and Star Wars across two parks.
There are 50+ attractions, more than 70 restaurants, and all of your favorite characters ready to snap a selfie with you throughout the parks!
Older kids will find lots to enjoy – like Big Thunder Mountain and Star Wars Hyperspace Mountain, to name just a couple. With themed attractions, parades, unreal meals, and adventures, every aspect of your experience is filled with Disney magic. 
The only question is: how long do you want to stay? But one thing's for sure – two parks are better than one, so make the most of your visit to Disneyland Paris®.

experiences.name: Sainte-Chapelle
experiences.description: Sainte-Chapelle is a Gothic-style royal chapel, known for its magnificent stained glass windows and for housing the Crown of Thorns. It is located on the Île de la Cité, in Paris.

experiences.name: Musée de l'Armée & Napoleon's Tomb
experiences.description:
The Musée de l'Armée is part of a complex of buildings, home to museums and monuments, all relating to France's military history. A visit provides a fascinating look at prestigious old weapons, uniforms and armour. Napoleon Bonaparte's gilded tomb is also here, so be sure to pay your respects. 
The Musée de l'Armée is located on Rue de Grenelle, at Paris's Les Invalides. It's a great spot in Paris’ 7th arrondissement, near Champs-de-Mars and the French National Assembly.

experiences.name: Paris Zoological Park (Parc Zoologique de Paris)
experiences.description:
Formerly known as the Bois de Vincennes Zoological Park, the 14.5-hectare Paris Zoological Park recently underwent two years of refurbishment works, giving this famous zoological park major natural environmental features, and plenty of room for the animals to roam. 
The animal habitats have been grouped five 'biozones', and the zoo has six restaurants and a 4,000 m2 tropical rainforest-filled greenhouse. It's visible from quite far away, thanks to the Big Rock, a 65m-tall artificial mini-mountain, which is an iconic symbol of the Paris Zoological Park.

experiences.name: Arc de Triomphe
experiences.description:
The Arc de Triomphe is Napoleon's Neoclassical take on the ancient Roman triumphal arch. It's at the center of a square, with a dozen Parisian avenues radiating from it into the rest of the City of Light. 
At the base of the arch, the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier is rekindled at 18:30 (6:30 pm) each evening.

experiences.name: Aquarium de Paris
experiences.description:
The Paris Aquarium is not only Europe's oldest aquarium (it was built in 1867), it was also Europe's largest for years. Housed in the center of the city – right next to the Eiffel Tower – on the quarries where Napoleon would house his cavalry, this institution has been part of the fabric of Parisian life for more than a century.
With 4 million liters of water, and 13,000 fish from around the world, there's plenty to keep you and your family entertained here for hours on end.

experiences.name: Musée Rodin Paris
experiences.description: The Musée Rodin in central Paris is dedicated to the life and work of French sculptor, Auguste Rodin. The French Impressionist sculptor crafted his later works in Hôtel Biron, which is now the Musée Rodin. Rodin donated much of his life's work to the French state, on condition that Hôtel Biron be converted into a museum celebrating his art.

experiences.name: Musée Marmottan Monet
experiences.description:
Musée Marmottan Monet is the best place on Earth to see Claude Monet's masterpieces up close. Visitors can admire over 100 of the Frenchman's works, donated by friends and family over many years, including iconic pieces like 'Impression, Sunrise' and a unique set of his 'Water lilies'.
It's not just Monet that makes the museum such a must-see in Paris. Your Musée Marmottan Monet tickets allow you to enjoy fellow Impressionist icons like Gauguin, Degas, and Morisot, as well as a calendar of highlight exhibitions.

experiences.name: Musée national Picasso-Paris
experiences.description:
This museum is located in a stunning 17th century hôtel particulier in the Marais district of Paris. It's dedicated, as the name suggests, to the great artist Pablo Picasso. Descendants of the Spanish-born artist sowed the seeds for this gallery with works they offered to the French government in lieu of inheritance tax. French law allows for, in exceptional circumstances, the payment of inheritance taxes with artworks instead of money. This exception is only made if the art is an important contribution to France's cultural heritage - which is of course true in the case of Picasso.
With a permanent collection, temporary exhibitions, and an in-depth archive, this is one of the most important collections of works by one of the 20th century's greatest artists.

experiences.name: La Cinémathèque Française
experiences.description:
Located in a breathtaking postmodern building designed by Frank Gehry, the Cinémathèque Française is a French non-profit film organization that houses one of the largest archives of film documents and film-related objects in the world.
Alongside daily showings of global cinemas on their film screens, the museum is also home to the Méliès Museum which showcases the life of French cinematic pioneer George Méliès.

experiences.name: Palais Garnier
experiences.description:
This 19th-century architectural masterpiece was built by Charles Garnier at the wishes of Emperor Napoleon III. It's located at the Place de l'Opera, a square in Paris's 9th arrondissement.
The insides of the Palais Garnier, also known as the Opéra Garnier, boast some stunning Beaux-Arts architecture, like the 30-m high marble Grand Staircase. Its intricate interior inspired Gaston Leroux's 1910 novel The Phantom of the Opera (and thus Andrew Lloyd Webber's smash-hit musical), making this the perhaps the most famous opera house in the world.

experiences.name: Eiffel Tower
experiences.description:
When the Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889, it was the tallest building in the world. With 18,000 metallic parts and 2.5 million rivets, it was a true feat of engineering – and the perfect centerpiece for that year’s World’s Fair. But Alexandre Gustave Eiffel's tower had as many haters as it had fans.
However, over time, the Eiffel Tower has become a universally beloved icon; it's now the most-visited paid tourist attraction in the world. The fact remains that no trip to Paris is complete without a visit to the Eiffel Tower.

experiences.name: Panthéon
experiences.description: The Panthéon is a beautiful, neo-Classical church located in the Latin Quarter of Paris, easy to reach on foot and by public transport. Originally dedicated to St. Genevieve (the patron saint of Paris), it's now a secular mausoleum that houses the remains of many French luminaries, Among those buried here are Voltaire, Rousseau, Victor Hugo, Marie Curie, and Émile Zola.

experiences.name: Musée de l'Orangerie
experiences.description:
In 1922 Claude Monet offered eight of his giant Water Lilies panels to the French state, as a way of commemorating the end of WWI. His only condition was that they be displayed in a suitable venue. The French government found the perfect spot: the Orangerie in the Tuileries Garden. 
The Orangerie was built in 1852 as a winter shelter for the orange trees that lined the garden of the Tuileries Palace. For a period of time it was also used as a storehouse for goods, a bunk for soldiers, and as a flexible exhibition space. Monet's generous gift transformed it into the Musée de l'Orangerie, a pre-eminent gallery of Impressionist and post-Impressionist paintings.
The huge (and hugely impressive) Monet paintings are definitely the main event, but they are far from the only reason to visit. The museum also contains works by Cézanne, Matisse, Modigliani, Picasso, Renoir, and Rousseau, among others.

experiences.name: Palais Galliera
experiences.description:
Palais Galliera is a Renaissance Revival building in Paris, France. Built in 1895, the grand palace has been home to Paris's fashion museum since 1977.
It now holds over 200,000 items including clothing, accessories, photos, and sketches, dating back as far as the 1700s. The museum hosts popular temporary exhibitions throughout the year.

experiences.name: Paris Hop-on Hop-off Tours
experiences.description:
Explore the magical city of Paris with a convenient hop-on hop-off bus tour! 
See the historic City of Light in all its glory, from the riverside to the bustling streets. You'll get an up-close look at the famous monuments, cobblestone alleys, and chic boutiques. 
Take in the sights and sounds of Paris while learning interesting facts from onboard commentary.

experiences.name: Musée Jacquemart-André
experiences.description: Private museum Jacquemart-André is located in Paris’s 8th arrondissement on the Boulevard Haussmann. It's easy to reach on metro stops Miromesnil, Monceau and Saint-Philipe-du-Roule and it's also within walking distance of the Champs-Élysées and Saint-Lazare train station.

experiences.name: Bourse de Commerce - Pinault Collection
experiences.description:
Bourse de Commerce - Pinault Collection is a contemporary art haven, the fruit of a collaboration between renowned art collector Francois Pinault and Pritzker Prize-winning architect Tadao Ando.
This 30,000-square-foot museum, a renovation of Paris's domed, 18th-century corn exchange, is one of the city's newest major museums, costing almost $200 million to realize. Hosting 10 or more temporary exhibitions per year, visitors can expect to see a wealth of famous works by the world's greatest contemporary artists, as well as high-profile loans from notable institutions around the globe.

experiences.name: Grévin Wax Museum
experiences.description:
At Grévin Wax Museum you’ll meet world-famous celebrities and fascinating figures. George Clooney and Franz Kafka, or Angelina Jolie, and the alchemy-loving emperor, Rudolf II and his many potions.
The museum also has some surprisingly hands-on exhibits. Kids will love the Discovery Tour where they can see how a wax figure is created. Touch wax and resin and see the fake ears and eyes that help bring a statue to life.

experiences.name: Musée du quai Branly - Jacques Chirac
experiences.description:
Suspended on a series of pillars, the Musée du quai Branly – Jacques Chirac features a vertical garden on the Seine side and enormous multicolored blocks on another. And once inside the grounds, the horizontal garden is full of birds and plants, walkways and paths. 
Inside the museum, it's an anthropological journey around the world via art and artifacts. The permanent collection reaches back as far as the Neolithic period, and spans Oceania, Asia, Africa and the America.

experiences.name: Aquaboulevard
experiences.description: The largest water park in Paris and one of the premier water parks in Europe, Aquaboulevard is the perfect family-friendly day out in Paris. The park features a wide array of waterslides, wave pools and other thrilling aquatic amenities, as well as more relaxed areas with huge jacuzzis and relaxation areas.

experiences.name: Atelier des Lumières
experiences.description: View impressive digital art projections. Attend special exhibits featuring famous artists.

experiences.name: Parc Astérix
experiences.description: Parc Astérix is an action-packed theme park based on the stories of the Asterix comics. Located just outside of Paris, this theme park is famous for its high-octane roller coasters and adrenaline-filled activities for thrill-seekers of all ages.

experiences.name: Ménagerie du Jardin des Plantes
experiences.description: Ménagerie du Jardin des Plantes is home to 1,200 animals, right in the center of Paris. It's one of the world's oldest zoos and also runs a breeding programme for endangered species. Visitors can mingle with mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians and other animals known as humans.

experiences.name: Grande Galerie de l'Évolution
