experiences.description: The Grande Galerie de l'Évolution is home to over 7,000 preserved animal specimens, all taking residence in a huge 19th-century hall in the light of a massive glass roof. It's an evolutionary adventure located in Paris, France.

experiences.name: Petit Palais
experiences.description:
The Petit Palais/Musée des Beaux-Arts de la Ville de Paris is a beautiful art museum in Paris located close to the Champs-Élysées – Clemenceau metro station.
View a diverse collection of fine arts, including paintings and sculptures. Attend temporary exhibitions that cover a wide range of artistic movements and styles.

experiences.name: Fondation Cartier pour l'Art Contemporain
experiences.description:
The Fondation Cartier pour l'Art Contemporain, based in Paris since 1994, resides in a transparent building designed by architect Jean Nouvel. 
Each year, the Fondation Cartier organizes thematic or solo exhibitions, commissioning artists to add to its extensive collection.

experiences.name: Louvre Museum
experiences.description:
The Louvre's magnificent classical silhouette (and funky glass pyramid) are well-known attractions in the center of Paris. But as nice as the Louvre is from the outside, its real magic is in the inside.
The Louvre's collection ranges from Mesopotamian, Egyptian, and Greek antiquities, with masterpieces by Da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Rembrandt. With 35,000 artifacts displayed at any given moment, it's a big, and worthwhile, commitment. 
The Louvre, known as the world's largest museum, is a cultural icon in Paris. A pre-booked, timed-entry ticket is now required for entry. This ensures you can explore its vast collection without long waits.

experiences.name: Château de Fontainebleau
experiences.description:
Château de Fontainebleau is just 55 kilometres from the center of Paris. This massive palace has over 1500 rooms sprawled out on 130 acres of gardens and parks. It's one of the largest royal French châteaux to have ever been built. 
Fontainebleau was much loved by Napoléon Bonaparte, who oversaw a lot of the restoration work. Napoléon III was another regular visitor, but as an example of the changes it has seen, during WWII the château was a headquarter for occupying Germans. It's now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

experiences.name: Musée Banksy Paris
experiences.description:
Musée Banksy is dedicated to the street-art movement and its elusive and enigmatic poster boy: Banksy. The exhibition features recreations of the artist's most iconic works by other street artists, and implores the viewer to think about the purpose and essence of what art is. 
If you are a fan of the cuttingly satirical art of Banksy, this is an absolute must.

experiences.name: Château de Chantilly
experiences.description: Château de Chantilly is a princely domain just a few kilometers outside of Paris, which provides a great look into the life and loves of French VIPs. The castle houses the Condé Museum and its princely apartments, an impressive collection of paintings, a library, and archives holding over 13,000 books, including the most valuable manuscript in the world.

experiences.name: Ballon de Paris Generali
experiences.description:
The Ballon de Paris Generali has been around since 1999 in the André Citroën Park (15th arrondissement), and flown over 500,000 passengers. 
The world's largest balloon also functions as an air quality indicator, so you can really find out what the air's like up there.

experiences.name: Musée National de la Marine
experiences.description:
At the Palais de Chaillot overlooking the Trocadéro Gardens, the Musée National de la Marine has reopened after six years of renovation. 
Learn France's maritime and naval history through a variety of interactive exhibits. From the days of wooden sailing ships to the age of modern cruise ships, you'll leave the museum with newfound knowledge about French maritime history.
Get ready to take a step back in time, taking a journey from Paris to Le Havre, braving storms and shipwrecks, and learn about 250 years of French maritime and naval history.

experiences.name: Jardin d'Acclimatation
experiences.description:
Jardin d'Acclimatation began as a French take on the English Garden way back in 1860. Once a temporary home to the animals brought back to France from the colonies, the modern Jardin d'Acclimatation retains much of its classic charm.
With over 40 family-fun attractions and over 400 animals on site, a day at Jardin d'Acclimatation has something for everyone. Enjoy rollercoasters, rides, and activities galore! Follow the squawks and chirps inside the Great Aviary, and stroll the park's grand 45-acre gardens.

experiences.name: Cité des sciences et de l'industrie
experiences.description: Paris's Cité des sciences et de l'industrie is the biggest science museum in Europe. Surrounded by a big green park, this science and tech center has a number of scientific attractions. Highlights include an IMAX theater, a planetarium, and the decommissioned submarine Argonaute.

experiences.name: Musée de la Libération de Paris - Musée du Général Leclerc - Musée Jean Moulin
experiences.description: The Musée de la Libération de Paris was inaugurated in 2019 to celebrate 75 years since the city was liberated from Nazi occupation. Hear the fascinating story of how proud, ordinary Parisians stood up to German rule and retook their city over six days in 1944.

experiences.name: Musée de l’Homme
experiences.description: The Musée de l'Homme is an anthropology museum located in Paris, France. It was first established in 1937 by Paul Rivet as part of the 1937 Exposition Internationale des Arts et Techniques dans la Vie Moderne.

experiences.name: Notre-Dame Cathedral of Paris
experiences.description: Observe the detailed Gothic architecture and stained glass windows. Climb the towers for a view of the city.

experiences.name: Musée National des Arts Asiatiques Guimet
experiences.description: View a large collection of Asian art and artifacts. Attend educational programs on Asian cultures and history.

experiences.name: Catacombs of Paris
experiences.description:
Easily one of the creepiest places on Earth, Les Catacombes de Paris (Catacombs of Paris) contains the remains of six million Parisians – that's almost three times the city's current population!
This macabre monument is in central Paris, and sometimes, right under your feet.

experiences.name: Carnavalet Museum
experiences.description: After four years of major renovation work, the Carnavalet Museum reopened its doors in May 2021. The oldest museum in Paris has had a real rebirth, so expect a breath of new life in a museum that's nearly 150 years old! You'll find more than 625,000 works that tell the story of Paris's past inside.

experiences.name: Bibliothèque nationale de France François-Mitterrand
experiences.description: Explore the extensive collection of books and manuscripts. Attend various exhibitions and cultural events held throughout the year.

experiences.name: Cité Immersive des Fables
experiences.description: Explore interactive exhibits and learn about different cultures. Attend workshops and participate in hands-on activities.

experiences.name: Château de Pierrefonds
experiences.description: Château de Pierrefonds is a magnificent castle designed and rebuilt in a medieval style by Napoleon III and his architect Eugène Viollet-le-Duc. It is located Pierrefonds, France, not too far by car from Paris.

experiences.name: ECLIPSO Paris
experiences.description:
Put on your VR headset with Eclipso to see historical scenes up close through engaging storytelling. 
You'll feel like you're traveling to different times and places, combining culture with new technology.

experiences.name: Monnaie de Paris
experiences.description: Monnaie de Paris, or the Paris Mint, is a government-owned institution responsible for producing France's euro coins. Founded in 864 AD, it is the world's oldest continuously-running minting institution.

experiences.name: Paris Cabaret Shows
experiences.description:
Experience the soul of Paris by booking a cabaret show and bask in the timeless spectacle of music, dance and comedy! Enjoy the allure of the city and the sparkle of the stage, as you watch a breathtaking display of top cabaret talent. 
Let a night of Parisian cabaret take you on a journey of enchantment, as you laugh and gasp at the glitzy, glamorous spectacle that has been entertaining the public in Paris for over a century. So don't wait, book your cabaret show today and let the show begin!

experiences.name: Château de Vaux-le-Vicomte
experiences.description: The Château de Vaux-le-Vicomte is a Baroque French château located 55 kilometers southeast of Paris. The château's ornate architecture and impressive manicured gardens are said to have been the inspiration for the construction of the more famous Palace of Versailles.

experiences.name: Musée des Arts et Métiers
experiences.description:
Musée des Arts et Métiers is a museum of industrial design in Paris, France, founded upon the principle of preserving groundbreaking artifacts of scientific importance.
Notable items in its vast collection include an original version of the Foucault pendulum, the original model of the Statue of Liberty, some of the first planes, and Blaise Pascal's Pascaline (the first mechanical calculator).

experiences.name: Paris Airport Transfers
experiences.description:
Make the most of your time in Paris by choosing a convenient and stress-free airport transfer option! Skip the long taxi lines, and enjoy a comfortable and reliable transfer from the airport to your destination. 
These airport transfers will ensure a hassle-free start to your stay in the City of Light. Once you've arrived, you'll be ready to explore the city and take in the sights. Book now and start your journey in style!

experiences.name: Hôtel de la Marine
experiences.description:
Hôtel de la Marine is a cultural icon in the heart of Paris. With a history stretching back to 1757, this grand monument on Place de la Concorde was home to Royal France's ever-moving collection of treasures and priceless furniture, before serving as the navy headquarters for almost 200 years. 
Only opened to the public in 2021 after a major refurbishment, the Hôtel de la Marine is a lesser-known cultural gem in Paris, just a 15-minute walk from the Louvre and Musée d'Orsay.

experiences.name: Paradox Museum Paris
experiences.description: Paradox Museum opens its doors in Paris, in a unique, giant space of 1700 m2 in the heart of the capital, a stone's throw from the Opera. The whole family will be immersed in Paradox Museum's array of visual, sensory and educational experiences. With more than 90 astonishing exhibitions inspired by paradoxes, the Paradox Museum Paris experiments with your senses and combines the world of science, art and human perception.

experiences.name: Safari de Thoiry
experiences.description:
Escape from the concrete jungle and return to nature at Safari de Thoiry, located just 35 minutes from Paris by car.
Hop in your car and drive an 8 km trail around the park and get up close to 800 different animals! You can even hop out of your vehicle and walk through a glass tunnel, which lets you see the animals up close – you might end up with a couple of lions lying on top of you!

experiences.name: Musée Cognacq-Jay
experiences.description: Musée Cognacq-Jay is a museum packed with 18th-century art and wonders in a classic French Renaissance mansion, located in the Marais District in Paris. The museum's collection was brought together by Ernest Cognacq and his wife Marie-Louise Jay between 1900 and 1925. This VIP couple had quite an influence on Paris, as they also founded the department store La Samaritaine, though they invested the majority of their fortune in French 18th-century works of art.

experiences.name: Conciergerie
experiences.description: The Conciergerie is located on the Île de la Cité, in Paris. This marvelous Gothic building preceded the Palais du Louvre as the royal seat of power in France but was later used as a prison during the French Revolution's Reign of Terror.

experiences.name: Maison Victor Hugo
experiences.description:
Standing alongside the likes of Shakespeare and Company, Café de Flore, and Oscar Wilde's grave at Père Lachaise Cemetery, Maison Victor Hugo is one of the most treasured literary pilgrimage sites in Paris. 
The luminary Romantic writer responsible for timeless masterpieces like Les Miserables, The Hunchback of Notre-Dame, and countless other works of genius, Victor Hugo spent 16 years of his incredible (and prolific) life in this sublime Parisian townhouse. 
These days the building is one of the 14 City of Paris Museums and is one of the best museums in Paris. And that's saying something!

experiences.name: Le Grand Rex
experiences.description: Le Grand Rex is a cinema and concert venue in Paris, France. A shining example of the Art Deco movement, Le Grand Rex is noted for its opulent decor and its enormous main auditorium. It also happens to be the largest cinema theater in Europe.

experiences.name: Notre Dame Space – Eternal Notre-Dame
experiences.description:
The Eternal Notre-Dame exhibition and experience is dedicated to bringing to life the past of the iconic landmark, the Notre Dame of Paris. 
With the aim of reviving the Notre Dame following the 2019 fire, this virtual reality show equips its visitors with immersive gear to time-travel as far back as the 13th century up to the 21st century on a tour of the monument's historical changes.

experiences.name: Musée de Montmartre
experiences.description: Immerse yourself in the artistic heritage of Paris' bohemian past, where artists like Renoir, Valadon, and Utrillo once lived and created their masterpieces. The museum's location, complete with gardens and views of the city, offers a retreat from the bustling streets of the Montmartre district.

experiences.name: Château de Vincennes
experiences.description:
At various times in its history, the Château de Vincennes has been a royal residence, a porcelain factory, a prison and a burial site. Its location was designed to allow the monarchy to keep a watchful eye over Paris. 
Now, with a metro stop named after it, it's a part of the French capital. Highlights include the highest fortified tower in Europe and the beautiful Gothic Sainte-Chapelle de Vincennes.

experiences.name: Dalí Paris
experiences.description: Dalí Paris is home to the biggest collection of Salvador Dalí's work in France, with over 300 pieces across its permanent collection. It is located in Montmartre, Paris, France.

experiences.name: Musée du Chocolat
experiences.description: Musée du Chocolat is a museum focused on the history of chocolate, from cacao harvesting to the final product. It's a lesson in indulgence.

experiences.name: Basilique Cathédrale de Saint-Denis
experiences.description: The Basilique Cathédrale de Saint-Denis is the world's first Gothic cathedral, completed in the 13th century. It's the final resting place for French royalty and the burial place of 43 kings, 32 queens and 10 servants to the monarchy.

experiences.name: Musée de la Vie Romantique
experiences.description: The Musée de la Vie Romantique is the place for falling in love... with contemporary art! It is located at the foot of Montmartre, in the 9th arrondissement of Paris, France. Spend some time starry-eyed in this 1830s hôtel particulier, which has studios, a greenhouse, a small garden, and a paved courtyard. Permanent collections are free.

experiences.name: SEA LIFE Paris Val d'Europe
experiences.description: SEA LIFE Paris Val d'Europe is a family-friendly exhibition space that's home to hundreds of sea creatures in an immersive setting. Kids will love touching starfish and rays in the touch pools, and watching the king penguins strutting their stuff. It is located in Serris, France.

experiences.name: Parrot World - The Immersive Animal Park
experiences.description: Experience the wonders of South America at Parrot World - the first immersive animal park in Île de France. Get up close to animals from the Amazon and Patagonia, including parrots, penguins, jaguars, and giant otters. Kids can enjoy a giant playground while you take a break in dedicated dining and picnic areas. And don't miss the immersive ranch, where you can meet alpacas, wallabies, and more. Come and discover the magic of South America at Parrot World!

experiences.name: Cité de l'Économie
experiences.description: Explore interactive exhibits about economic concepts. Attend workshops and presentations on financial literacy.

experiences.name: Paris Walking Tours
experiences.description:
Explore the beauty and culture of Paris on an engaging walking tour! Take a stroll through the City of Light and explore the iconic sights and hidden gems of this remarkable city. Discover centuries-old architecture, taste the delicious local delicacies and get immersed in the city's vibrant culture. 
These walking tours are the perfect way to get to know the city and see the sights up close. With knowledgeable tour guides, you'll learn all about the history and culture of Paris. So why wait? Book your Paris walking tour now and create an unforgettable experience!

experiences.name: Giverny Day trips from Paris
experiences.description:
Take a day trip from Paris to the famous French impressionist village of Giverny. Enjoy a peaceful journey through the picturesque French countryside, and explore the charming village with its colorful gardens and stunning architecture. 
Get a unique perspective of the local sights and soak up the atmosphere of this beautiful destination. And don't forget to take lots of photos to capture the beauty of this place!

experiences.name: Paris Self-Guided Tours
experiences.description: Explore Paris at your own pace with the self-guided tours. Choose from a range of options, including walking tours, bike tours, and museum tours, and discover the city's rich history, art, and culture.

experiences.name: Le Palais de Tokyo
experiences.description: Paris's rule-breaking museum showcases only the most cutting-edge of modern art, with regular programs of exhibitions sure to delight, inspire and provoke in equal measure.

experiences.name: Jeu de Paume
experiences.description: Nestled in the heart of the Tuileries Garden lies the historic Jeu de Paume, an iconic cultural institution and artistic hub, dedicated to showcasing images from the 20th and 21st centuries. This includes photography, video, contemporary art, cinema, and online creations. Through its curated exhibitions, film cycles, seminars, educational activities, and publications, Jeu de Paume seeks not only to highlight the work of renowned artists such as Richard Avedon, Diane Arbus, and Sally Mann, but also to introduce emerging talents like Omer Fast and Ali Cherri. Each exhibition tells a unique story, whether historical or contemporary, and attracts an increasingly diverse audience. Jeu de Paume is more than an art center

experiences.name: The Archeological Crypt of the Île de la Cité
experiences.description: The archaeological crypt of the Island City is a museum of the city of Paris, just beneath the parvis of Notre Dame Cathedral. It presents archaeological vestiges of antiquity, to the 19th century, discovered during excavations between 1965 and 1972.

experiences.name: Paris City Cards
experiences.description:
Explore the City of Lights with the Paris City Card, the ultimate ticket to discovering the beauty of Paris. Take advantage of great savings on some of the best things to do in Paris, from iconic attractions to unique local experiences. 
See world-famous masterpieces, explore hidden gardens, and take a culinary journey through the city's best restaurants. With so much to explore and experience, the Paris City Card is the perfect way to make the most of your time in Paris.

experiences.name: AccroCamp Créteil
experiences.description: Climb various rope courses and zip lines. Challenge different obstacle courses designed for all skill levels.

experiences.name: Père Lachaise Cemetery
experiences.description: With more than 3.5 million visitors every year, Père Lachaise Cemetery is the most visited graveyard in the world. The list of notable historical figures laid to rest here is expansive and features icons like Oscar Wilde, Jim Morrison, Edith Piaf, Marcel Proust, Gertrude Stein, Chopin, Pierre Bourdieu, and George-Eugène Haussmann – just to name a few. It's located in the 20th arrondissement of Paris.

experiences.name: Geology and Mineralogy Gallery
experiences.description: The Mineralogy and Geology Gallery is a part of the National Museum of Natural History. It is situated in the Jardin des Plantes. The gallery displays a great collection of crystals, gemstones and minerals.

experiences.name: Bibliothèque nationale de France Richelieu
experiences.description: Explore the extensive collections of books, manuscripts, and historical documents. Attend exhibitions showcasing art, history, and culture.

experiences.name: Cité de l'architecture et du patrimoine
experiences.description: Cité de l'architecture et du patrimoine (Architecture and Heritage City) is a museum for all lovers of architecture, whether they're new to the subject or if it's their passion and profession. Casts and miniatures of major French monuments from the 12th century to the present day are available for guests to admire, as well as a number of international buildings of great renown from the 19th century onwards.

experiences.name: Bruges Day Trips from Paris
experiences.description:
Take a break from the hustle and bustle of Paris with a day trip to Bruges! Explore the picturesque canals, cobblestone streets and charming buildings of this beautiful city in northern Belgium. 
Discover all of its cultural attractions and historical landmarks, and don't forget to treat yourself to some of the delicious local cuisine. With a day trip to Bruges, you can rest assured that you'll have a memorable experience.

experiences.name: Champagne Day Trips from Paris
experiences.description:
Experience the classically sophisticated allure of champagne from the unparalleled comfort of a day excursion departing from beautiful Paris. 
Savor the distinct notes of France's renowned bubbly in the very vineyards where its cherished grapes are meticulously grown. 
Between sips, marvel at the enchanting charm of the rustic countryside, ensuring this trip becomes a memory to fondly celebrate.

experiences.name: Versailles Day Trips from Paris
experiences.description:
Venture into the regal splendor of a Versailles day trip from Paris, the perfect escape teeming with awe-inspiring grandeur! 
Lose yourself amid lavish gardens, extravagant rooms, and opulent halls, drenched in a rich swirl of history and luxury that leaves every visitor in awe. 
With each step, the bygone era's stories unweave, creating an unforgettable journey that you'll fondly recount for years to come.

experiences.name: Mont Saint-Michel Day Trips from Paris
experiences.description: Embark on a magical day trip from Paris to the stunning Mont Saint-Michel. Marvel at the architectural wonder, enjoy panoramic views, and discover the rich history of this iconic landmark.

experiences.name: Villa Savoye
experiences.description: Villa Savoye is the masterpiece of Swiss architect Charles-Édouard Jeanneret, aka Le Corbusier. Commonly cited as the basis for modern architecture, Villa Savoye, found in Poissy, France, is a classic example of Jeanneret's five points of architecture. The villa, less than an hour's journey from Paris, was built in 1929 and is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

experiences.name: Cité Internationale de la Langue Française – Château de Villers-Cotterêts
experiences.description:
Nestled within the renovated walls of the Renaissance-era Villers-Cotterêts Castle, built by François I in 1532, the Cité Internationale de la Langue Française is a vibrant, living tribute to the French language. 
Dedicated to sharing and fostering a love for this ever-evolving language, this venue stretches over 1200m2 and offers your a rich cultural experience! You'll visit temporary exhibition halls, a performance room, courtyards, gardens, a café, and a bookstore. Consider this your open invitation to immerse yourself in French culture, right in the heart of the unique green haven that is the Retz Forest.

experiences.name: France Miniature
experiences.description: France Miniature lets visitors uncover the diverse regions of France by walking around the sites as a giant might! See a miniature Eiffel Tower, the Château Vaux le Vicomte, Saint-Tropez, Mont Saint-Michel, the Arles Amphitheatre, the Rosary Basilica at Lourdes, and other French landmarks in one park. There are also rides, slides and play areas for kids, plus two family restaurants on-site. France Miniature is located in Élancourt, France.

experiences.name: Roland Garros Stadium
experiences.description: The Roland-Garros Stadium is the home of the French Open. The 34-acre complex contains twenty courts and is arguably the most famous clay court tournament in the world of competitive tennis.

experiences.name: Perfume Museum
experiences.description:
Set in the heart of Paris, just a stone’s throw from the Opéra Garnier, the Musée du Parfum is a one-of-a-kind, fun and instructive museum concept, that brings perfume to life. 
Inside, you'll follow the manufacturing process from raw materials to harvesting, extraction, distillation, formulation, industrialization and bottling. There's also the chance to find out more about the creative process and the master noses required to create scents. The second section of the museum features an exceptional collection of ancient bottles retracing the history of fragrance from Ancient Egypt to the 20th century, including kohl pots, pomanders, vinaigrettes, perfume burners, pots-pourris, travel sets, smelling salt bottles and precious flasks.

experiences.name: Les Caves du Louvre
experiences.description: Les Caves du Louvre is an 18th-century wine cellar that was once used by the King of France. Now, it's a pleasurable multisensory experience dedicated to informing visitors about the art (and science) of winemaking and wine-tasting. Distinguish a Chardonnay from a Chablis, a Beaujolais from a Burgundy, and everything in-between!

experiences.name: Stade de France
experiences.description: The Stade de France is the national stadium of France, located in the commune of Saint-Denis, just north of Paris. The stadium is used by the national football team and rugby union team for international competitions. Its seating capacity of 80,698 makes it the 8th-largest stadium in Europe.

experiences.name: Château de Rambouillet
experiences.description: Château de Rambouillet is a beautiful home and former retreat for presidents, kings and VIPs in the town of Rambouillet, in the Île-de-France region of northern France. A visit here is a step back in time to see how some of the greatest men (and women) in history lived.

experiences.name: Paris Photoshoots
experiences.description:
Make your dreams come true with a photoshoot in Paris! Capture the romance of the City of Light with a professional photoshoot. Stroll through the cobblestone streets, explore the lush parks and gardens and take in the iconic architecture - all while a professional photographer captures your special moments. 
Get the perfect souvenir from your trip to Paris and create memories that will last a lifetime. Book your photoshoot today and see Paris like never before!

experiences.name: Paris Bike Tours
experiences.description:
Paris is one of the most beautiful cities in the world, and a bike tour is the perfect way to explore it! Experience the sights, sounds, and smells of Paris from a unique and exciting perspective. 
These bike tours of Paris give you the opportunity to discover the city's hidden gems and iconic landmarks, all at your own pace. 
With a knowledgeable local guide, you'll have the chance to explore Paris like never before.

experiences.name: Musée Yves Saint Laurent Paris
experiences.description: Musée Yves Saint Laurent Paris is located in the iconic Hôtel Particulier, where Yves Saint Laurent spent almost 30 years designing his high fashion collections from 1974 to 2002.

experiences.name: Château de Champs-sur-Marne
experiences.description: The Château de Champs-sur-Marne is an 18th-century château roughly 25 kilometers east of Paris. The château boasts an 85-hectare garden and rococo style decor. It can be accessed by public transport with a journey time of roughly 40 minutes from Paris.

experiences.name: SUMMIT One Vanderbilt
experiences.description:
SUMMIT One Vanderbilt is New York City’s most innovative observation space. Set 1,000 feet above Midtown, this experience will change the way you see the city, the environment, and yourself through a collection of immersive, multi-sensory experiences. 
Spread across 65,000 ft2 (6,000 m2) and three levels, enjoy unbeatable views of New York and interactive artistic installations that will stay with you long after you've left.
As you plan your visit, decide between daytime, sunset, or nighttime entry. Each option promises a unique perspective, creating a distinct atmosphere for exploration. Whether you're drawn to the clarity of daylight, the transition of colors at sunset, or the captivating city lights at night, SUMMIT invites you to choose your own adventure.

experiences.name: Edge NYC
experiences.description:
Edge at Hudson Yards is a 1,100-ft observation deck located in Hudson Yards, New York. 
The skyscraper boasts the highest outdoor observation deck in the Western Hemisphere, with a modern and unique design. It is roughly a 1-min walk from Vessel.

experiences.name: The Museum of Modern Art (MoMA)
experiences.description: The MoMA has one of the largest and most influential collections of modern art in the world. Home to famous works like Vincent Van Gogh's Starry Night, Salvador Dali's The Persistence of Memory, and works by Mondrian, Monet, and Picasso, this is a haven for art lovers. Located smack in the center of Midtown Manhattan, MoMA is a must-see destination in New York.

experiences.name: New York Aquarium
experiences.description: The New York Aquarium is the oldest of its kind in the US – it opened in 1896! It's located on the famous Coney Island boardwalk and hosts a variety of marine mammals like seals and otters, as well as jellyfish, sharks, penguins, and hundreds of other aquatic animals.

experiences.name: Bronx Zoo
experiences.description: The Bronx Zoo, as you may not be surprised to learn, is in the Bronx. It's the largest metropolitan zoo in the US and one of the biggest in the world. Highlights include the Congo Gorilla Forest and the African Plains, and the Children's Zoo is a perennial hit with the little ones.

experiences.name: Top of the Rock
experiences.description:
Depending on how old you are, you might know 30 Rockefeller Plaza as the RCA, GE, or Comcast building, but most folks know it as 30 Rock.
Commissioned by John D. Rockefeller, aka the richest person in modern history, this Art Deco beauty is 850 ft (260 m) tall and it's located in the very heart of Manhattan. 30 Rock was designed as the centerpiece of the Rockefeller Center, which comprises 19 buildings and what are probably the most famous Christmas trees and ice skating rink in the world.
As well as the NBC Studios and the venerable Rainbow Room restaurant, 30 Rock has an excellent observation deck called the Top of the Rock.

experiences.name: Splish Splash Water Park
experiences.description: Splish Splash is New York’s Largest Waterpark, offering 96 acres of wave pools, water slides, lazy river rides and more at Long Island’s premier family destination.

experiences.name: American Museum of Natural History (AMNH)
experiences.description: One of the largest museums in the world, the American History Museum is located across the street from Central Park. The museum complex consists of 27 interconnected buildings housing 45 permanent exhibition halls, in addition to a planetarium and a library. The museum collections contain over 32 million plant, human, animal, fossil, mineral, rock, and meteorite specimens - as well as human cultural artifacts. The museum has been featured in numerous TV shows and movies - most notably Night at the Museum and Night at the Museum 2.

experiences.name: New York Botanical Garden
experiences.description: Founded in 1891 and now a National Historic Landmark, the New York Botanical Garden is the largest botanical garden in any city in the United States. It's also a well-known plant research and conservation society.

experiences.name: Old Westbury Gardens
experiences.description:
Old Westbury Gardens, listed on the National Register of Historic Places, is the former home of John S. Phipps, his wife, Margarita Grace Phipps and their four children. 
Completed in 1906 by the English designer, George A. Crawley, the magnificent Charles II-style mansion is nestled amid 200 acres of formal gardens, landscaped grounds, woodlands, ponds and lakes. 
Westbury House is furnished with fine English antiques and decorative arts from the more than 50 years of the family's residence.

experiences.name: Statue of Liberty
experiences.description: The Statue of Liberty National Monument and Ellis Island are located in New York Harbor. Both can be reached by ferry departing from Battery Park in New York City or Liberty State Park in New Jersey.

experiences.name: Central Park Zoo
experiences.description:
Central Park Zoo, built in 1859, is a 6.5-acre zoo in Central Park in New York City. Initially a humble menagerie, displaying a few swans and a bear, you can observe a multitude of exotic animals up close. 
You'll see playful penguins, majestic snow leopards, and colorful tropical birds as you walk through lush gardens and learn about the wildlife conservation efforts of the zoo. 
Bring your camera to capture unforgettable moments with the animals. A trip to Central Park Zoo will be a fun and educational experience for you and your family.

experiences.name: Empire State Building
experiences.description: Completed in 1931, the Empire State Building is located in the heart of midtown Manhattan at 5th Avenue and 34th Street. Boasting a gorgeous Art Deco-style design, the Empire State Building still the 5th tallest skyscraper in the United States.

experiences.name: New York City Cards
experiences.description:
New York, New York – a city so good, they named it twice! But if you want to see the city that never sleeps and save time and money too, it's best to combine your tickets! 
New York city cards offer steep discounts on both admission fees and transport around the city – the Empire State Building, Times Square, 9/11 Memorial, and Ellis Island are all within reach. 
Access the Big Apple's top attractions with flexibility and convenience with options good for several days, or pack several sights into a shorter trip.

experiences.name: The 9/11 Memorial & Museum
experiences.description:
Architects Michael Arad and Peter Walker designed the 9/11 Memorial. Their proposal, called Reflecting Absence, consists of two reflecting pools that sit in the footprints of the South and North Towers. They're fed by waterfalls (the largest manmade waterfalls in the United States) and surrounded by a forest of nearly 400 trees.
The accompanying 9/11 Museum has an exhibition divided into three sections, the Day of 9/11, Before 9/11, and After 9/11. It uses artifacts, photos, and an extensively researched and well-laid-out collection to show how the events of 9/11 changed the world.

experiences.name: Museum of Illusions New York
experiences.description: Interact with optical illusions and mind-bending exhibits. Challenge perception with hands-on activities and puzzles.

experiences.name: Six Flags New England
experiences.description:
From the tip of the New England SkyScreamer to the bottom of the Tea Cups, there's excitement for every family member at the awesome Six Flags New England.
Six Flags New England is home to 13 pant-wetting coasters, including one of the top-rated steel coasters on the planet, SUPERMAN The Ride. The park is located in Agawam, Massachusetts, a western suburb of Springfield, Massachusetts.
Get your Six Flags New England tickets today and enter a world of family fun and adventurous things to do in New England!

experiences.name: New York Sightseeing Cruises
experiences.description:
Swap the hustle and bustle of the New York City streets for the harbors of the Hudson River with tickets for an NYC boat tour. 
Scan the iconic Manhattan skyline from the unique perspective of the water, which is even more dazzling during a sunset cruise. Elevate your New York state of mind with a dinner cruise featuring fresh entrees, cocktails, and live music, as you enjoy the benefits of a city that never sleeps.
With so much to see in so little time, the pre-selected NYC sightseeing day cruises eliminate the guesswork so you can see the best of NYC in a New York minute. Sail by the Statue of Liberty, Ellis Island, and the Brooklyn Bridge with an expert tour guide to help you take a bite out of Big Apple history.
There are plenty of NYC boat tours to choose from – scan the page and take your pick!

experiences.name: Museum of Ice Cream New York
experiences.description:
Guys, there's a swimming pool of rainbow sprinkles here!
If that's not enough to get you to NYC's Museum of Ice Cream, we're not sure if a three-story tunnel slide, free tastings, or a perfectly pink subway car are going to change your mind. Come and get a flavor of New York's ode to happiness!

experiences.name: One World Observatory
experiences.description:
Designed by architectural firm Skidmore, Owings & Merrill - who also designed the Burj Khalifa - One World is the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere and the sixth tallest building in the world. 
The One World Observatory - located on the 100th, 101st and 102nd floors of the Freedom Tower - doesn’t stop bringing the 'wow'; from the moment you enter the building, to its elevator rides, all the way up to the top of New York City.

experiences.name: ARTECHOUSE New York
experiences.description:
ARTECHOUSE New York takes state-of-the-art exhibitions to the next level! Far from your usual museum, this immersive art experience places cutting-edge technology and science at the heart of its visitor journey, making it one of the most unique artistic destinations in New York.
Beyond just looking at the art, you'll physically become a part of it, as incredible experiential exhibitions turn theoretical physics and mind-warping math into stunning, interactive light art.
Exhibitions vary throughout the year but are always guaranteed to leave you wondering about the relationship between art and science, and the essential role of the observer in each field.

experiences.name: Sea Life Aquarium - New Jersey
experiences.description: SEA LIFE New Jersey is an aquarium located in the American Dream Mall in East Rutherford, New Jersey. It is located beside the Legoland® Discovery Center inside the mall.

experiences.name: Cathedral of Saint John the Divine
experiences.description: The Cathedral of Saint John the Divine is a Romanesque and Gothic Revival landmark in Morningside Heights and remains a New York treasure. It's home to the largest Rose Window in the US and is one of the five largest church buildings in the world.

experiences.name: Museum of Sex
experiences.description: Having opened its doors in NYC in 2000, the Museum of Sex has presented over 40 exhibitions in collaboration with leading academics and scholars, and in 2019 launched Super Funland: Journey into the Erotic Carnival, a permanent, four-floor immersive carnival experience. Winner of the 2022 Traveler's Choice Award from TripAdvisor, and receiving upwards of 4 stars on both Google & TripAdvisor, Museum of Sex has been delighting customers for decades. Visitors agree it offers the perfect combination of education and fun. Go check out the Museum, as well as its award winning retail store and bar.

experiences.name: Intrepid Museum
experiences.description:
A museum housed on an aircraft carrier that happens to also be a national historic monument. The USS Intrepid actually served in World War II and Vietnam and helped out NASA in recovery missions. 
Stacked with cool aircraft, including the Lockheed A-12 Blackbird and the Concord, plus space shuttles. It’s docked right on the Hudson River, waiting for you to come aboard and enter the Danger Zone.

experiences.name: The Guggenheim
experiences.description:
There's no mistaking this incomparable gallery/museum. Designed by master architect Frank Lloyd Wright, the Guggenheim's sloping, spiraling walls house an unbeatable collection of modern art, focusing on the 20th century and beyond!
The Guggenheim in New York has one of the finest collections of modern art in the world. Art lovers shouldn't pass up the chance to visit!

experiences.name: New York Helicopter Tours
experiences.description:
See New York City from above on a helicopter tour.  
Fly over the Manhattan skyline, city streets, and harbor with a professional pilot guiding the way.

experiences.name: Broadway Shows New York
experiences.description:
Experience the thrill of New York theater with a Broadway show! 
See world-class talent, dazzling sets, and first-rate production quality live and in-person. 
Feel the energy of a full theater, and discover why theater-lovers travel from all over the world to experience the best of Broadway. 
Get your tickets now and join the millions of people who have already seen a show in the Big Apple.

experiences.name: Mercer Labs
experiences.description:
Step into the future at MERCER LABS - NYC’s newest must-see museum blending art and technology!   Limitless by Roy Nachum redefines the museum experience through fifteen interactive exhibition spaces, infinity rooms, unique 4D Sound encounters and immersive installations. Mercer Labs redefines the relationship between art and technology. 
·       The multi-sensory immersive museum features colossal projections, stunning infinity rooms and the largest installation of its kind - The Dragon Room with over half a million LEDs creating an unparalleled visual experience
·       Immerse yourself in the most unique 4D Sound gallery in the world with omni-directional sound that renders vivid space sound holograms with precision and clarity
·       An interactive Drawing Station allows guests to scan their personalized animal creations into a live 3D digital video ecosystem

Featuring artist Roy Nachum's Crown Kids

experiences.name: QC NY SPA
experiences.description: Experience year-round access to outdoor panoramic spa pools just a 5-minute ferry ride away from downtown Manhattan. Situated on Governors Island, the QC NY Spa offers a relaxing retreat with stunning views of the iconic city skyline. Treat yourself to a personalized massage or explore more than 20 rejuvenating wellness spa offerings. QC NY SPA is open every day until 10 pm from Sunday to Thursday and until 11 pm on Friday and Saturday.

experiences.name: Madame Tussauds New York
experiences.description: New York City's Madame Tussauds is located on Times Square. This world-famous museum of waxworks is one of New York's most unique attractions, featuring five floors of interactive entertainment.

experiences.name: RiseNY
experiences.description: RiseNY is a 4D virtual tour ride of New York City.

experiences.name: Whitney Museum of American Art
experiences.description:
The Whitney is a beautiful museum dedicated to the works of American artists in the twentieth and twenty first century. Named after founder and socialite Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney the museum's collection of new and innovative American contemporary art is unmatched. 
Its entrance is on the southern end of High Line Park, so you can combine your cultural outing with some frisbee and a picnic.

experiences.name: Arte Museum New York
experiences.description: View contemporary art installations and digital exhibitions. Participate in interactive workshops and events.

experiences.name: Madison Square Garden
experiences.description: Madison Square Garden is a huge entertainment complex/arena in New York City's Manhattan. It's located in Midtown Manhattan between 7th and 8th Avenues, from 31st to 33rd Streets, atop Pennsylvania Station.

experiences.name: New York Bike Rentals
experiences.description:
Explore the vibrant city of New York on two wheels! 
Get ready to experience the city like never before with a bike rental. Soak up the sights and sounds of this bustling metropolis without getting stuck in traffic. 
Whether you're a first-time visitor to the Big Apple or a local New Yorker, this is the perfect way to get around and explore.

experiences.name: Vessel (Hudson Yards Staircase)
experiences.description: The Thomas Heatherwick–designed structure known as Vessel is located in Manhattan's Hudson Yards. It's the biggest private real-estate project in American history designed for mixed use.

experiences.name: Complete Playground
experiences.description: Slide down the tall slides and climb the various play structures. Enjoy a picnic at the shaded tables and watch children play.

experiences.name: New York City Tours
experiences.description:
Take a city tour of New York to see its historic landmarks, vibrant culture, and unique attractions.  
Visit top sites across the city, from busy streets to amazing skyline views.  
Explore New York City and discover why it’s called the city that never sleeps.

experiences.name: New York Bike Tours
experiences.description:
Explore New York City on two wheels! 
These New York City bike tours offer an exciting way to discover the Big Apple, giving you the freedom to find your own adventure. 
Your experienced tour guides will lead the way, showing you the sights and sounds of the city in a new light.

experiences.name: Color Factory NYC
experiences.description: The Color Factory NYC is the East Coast sister of San Francisco's original pop-up museum: a sensual exploration of the Big Apple's hues, tones and shades, based in the SoHo district of Manhattan.

experiences.name: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
experiences.description: The Metropolitan Museum of Art, also known as The Met, opened on February 20, 1872. The original building comprised of red brick and stone, but has since been updated with a facade in the Beaux-Arts style. It is located on 5th Avenue and 82nd Street, right by Central Park.

experiences.name: New York Hop-on Hop-off Tours
experiences.description:
New York City is packed with sightseeing opportunities. The best way to see it all? A Hop-on Hop-off Tour of New York.  
See the city from the water and scoot between the waterfront landmarks with the breezy Water Taxi NY, hopping on and off to explore when something catches your eye. Or get the full Hudson sightseeing experience with a fact-packed sightseeing cruise, and drift by the iconic Manhattan skyline, the Statue of Liberty, Ellis Island, and more. 
Back on land, give the big yellow taxis a miss and jump aboard a sightseeing NYC bus. These ultra-convenient sightseeing tours are designed to take the guesswork – and the legwork! – out of exploring New York's many must-see highlights.

experiences.name: Great Jones Distilling Company
experiences.description:
Visit Manhattan’s first whiskey distillery since Prohibition at Great Jones Distilling Co., located at 686 Broadway in the NoHo neighborhood. A century after the last distillery closed, whiskey production has returned to the city.
The distillery uses 100% New York-sourced ingredients in its whiskeys. Visitors can tour the facility, enjoy a cocktail, or have a meal with local ingredients at the onsite restaurant.

experiences.name: Public Transport New York
experiences.description:
With an NYC public transport ticket in hand, New York City becomes your playground! 
Eficiently navigate through the city's vast transportation network – as you move from one location to another, you'll get an authentic taste of the city's energetic culture.

experiences.name: Yankee Stadium
experiences.description:
Welcome to Yankee Stadium, the iconic home of the New York Yankees! This legendary ballpark is situated in the bustling Bronx neighborhood of New York City and has been the site of countless historic moments in baseball history.
Since its opening in 2009, Yankee Stadium has been a beacon of greatness for baseball fans around the world. With a seating capacity of over 50,000, this state-of-the-art stadium offers an unparalleled game-day experience. From the moment you step through the gates, you'll be struck by the grandeur and spectacle of this world-renowned venue.
Whether you're a die-hard fan or simply looking to experience one of the most iconic sporting venues in the world, Yankee Stadium is a must-see destination. Come witness the thrill of the game and be a part of history at this one-of-a-kind venue.

experiences.name: SPYSCAPE
experiences.description: This cutting-edge espionage museum lets you become a real-world James Bond, practicing spy-skills like interrogation, computer hacking, and infiltration, then seeing how you can apply them in your everyday life – you'll be shaken and stirred.

experiences.name: St. Patrick's Cathedral
experiences.description:
Named for Ireland's patron saint, St. Patrick's Cathedral symbolizes religious freedom for the immigrants who flocked to New York during the late 19th and 20th centuries. 
The cathedral is located on the east side of Fifth Avenue, directly opposite the Rockefeller Center.

experiences.name: The Museum of Broadway
experiences.description: The Museum of Broadway is a new immersive theatrical experience in the heart of Times Square that takes visitors on a journey from the birth of Broadway to the present day. The museum highlights groundbreaking moments in Broadway’s history through a series of exhibits that showcase – and show off – spectacular costumes, props, renderings, rare photos, videos, and more. Additionally, the Museum of Broadway celebrates the dazzling American art form of musical theater and the people who make it come to life.

experiences.name: American Dream Observation Wheel
experiences.description: Dream Wheel is a modern Ferris wheel in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Take a sping on the Dream Wheel by Skyviews at American Dream for a amazing views of the Manhattan Skyline, VIP services and more.

experiences.name: Round trips from New York to Woodbury Common Premium Outlets
Luggage Storage New York
experiences.description:
Traveling to New York City? You won't want heavy bags slowing you down, so why not store your luggage and keep it safe while you explore? 
Drop off your bags and start discovering iconic attractions like Times Square, Central Park, and the Statue of Liberty without the extra weight. Conveniently located and easy to use, this service lets you make the most of your trip. Enjoy the city hands-free!

experiences.name: New York Historical Society
experiences.description: The New York Historical Society was New York's first museum, founded in 1804. This museum and library is crammed with American history and art, plus changing exhibitions with a focus on American arts and achievements. You'll find it on the corner of 77th Street and Central Park West in Manhattan.

experiences.name: HERO at Rockefeller Center
experiences.description:
HERO, located inside the Rockefeller Center, is a new venue across from the skating rink dedicated to visual and sonic storytelling. Inside, you'll find an immersive experience showcasing the work of a dozen artists and designers.
Through engaging video, spatial audio, and scenic design, they've created a dreamscape that transports you from the hustle of New York City. Guests can explore the exhibitions and enjoy the immersive experience at their own pace.

experiences.name: Sloomoo Institute
experiences.description:
Immerse yourself in the joy of sensory play. Our #satisfying world is full of never-ending, hand-crafted slime, yummy scents, soothing ASMR delights, and lots of surprises. Amazing for families and adults, Forbes calls us a “cornucopia for the senses.” Today Show said it is a “mega dream for all ages.”
A custom 8 oz. slime is included with every ticket! Wear casual clothes in case you get slimey. Get off your screens and escape with us!

experiences.name: Rockefeller Center
experiences.description:
Nestled in the heart of New York City, the iconic Rockefeller Center stands tall as a symbol of grandeur and cultural significance. With its rich history and captivating allure, this sprawling complex offers an unparalleled experience for locals and tourists alike.
Spanning 22 acres, Rockefeller Center is a masterpiece of architectural brilliance. Its Art Deco design, meticulously crafted during the 1930s, exudes a timeless elegance that has enchanted visitors for decades. The center is comprised of 19 magnificent buildings, including the renowned Radio City Music Hall, the RCA Building (also known as the GE Building), and the legendary 30 Rockefeller Plaza, which is affectionately referred to as "30 Rock."
A hub of vibrant activity, Rockefeller Center offers a myriad of attractions and activities to engage the senses. The Top of the Rock observation deck affords breathtaking panoramic views of the city.

experiences.name: Broadway Comedy Club
experiences.description: Watch live comedy shows featuring both seasoned and up-and-coming comedians. Enjoy a variety of comedic styles and performances in an intimate setting.

experiences.name: Nassau County Museum of Art
experiences.description: The Nassau County Museum of Art has a strong focus on encouraging a deeper understanding of art and culture through a range of exhibitions and educational programs for people of all ages and backgrounds. The museum is popular for its excellent permanent collection, sculpture park, and tranquil natural setting.

experiences.name: Hard Rock Cafe New York
experiences.description:
Located in the center of New York City and the heart of Times Square, Hard Rock Cafe New York will rock your senses with tantalizing food and drinks and an awe inspiring collection of music memorabilia. 
Times Square, also known as the “Crossroads of the World,” is home to the New Years’ Eve Ball Drop, Broadway Theatre, Madame Tussauds wax museum, and many other famous attractions. 
The fun, food and excitement never end in Hard Rock Cafe New York. Located in one of the grandest entertainment destinations in the city,  the cafe also boasts a unique outdoor space above the building’s historic marquee.

experiences.name: LEGOLAND® Discovery Center New Jersey
experiences.description: LEGOLAND® Discovery Center New Jersey is located inside the American Dream mall and features over 10 LEGO-themed play zones, including a miniature New York and New Jersey, made from over a million LEGO bricks!

experiences.name: One Times Square
experiences.description: Watch the New Year's Eve ball drop event. See the iconic digital billboards and advertisements.

experiences.name: LEGOLAND® Discovery Center Westchester
experiences.description: LEGOLAND® Discovery Center Westchester is a family entertainment center located at Ridge Hill Shopping Center in Westchester County, New York. The center features LEGO®-themed rides, a play area, a 4D cinema, creative building workshops, and a gift shop.

experiences.name: Radio City Music Hall
experiences.description: The Radio City Music Hall is an Art Deco-style New York landmark, built in 1932. Its interiors are still considered some of the most impressive in the history of modern theater design. The hall is home to the Rockettes and functions as a year-round entertainment venue, located in Midtown Manhattan close to several subway lines. It's nicknamed 'The Showplace of the Nation'.

experiences.name: Museum of the City of New York
experiences.description:
The Museum of the City of New York encourages all visitors to explore the distinctive nature of urban life in the world's most influential metropolis. It's a fully interactive experience that draws you in by celebrating, documenting, and interpreting the city's past, present, and future.
The Museum of the City of New York is a must-see for travelers to New York. It's located on 5th Ave, New York, NY.

experiences.name: ECLIPSO NYC
experiences.description: Browse through unique immersive exhibits and enjoy a variety of virtual reality programs.

experiences.name: Frederick Phineas and Sandra Priest Rose Center for Earth and Space
experiences.description: The Rose Center for Earth and Space encompasses the spectacular Hayden Sphere and exhibitions that explore the vast range of sizes in the cosmos, the 13-billion-year history of the universe, the nature of galaxies, stars, and planets, and the dynamic features of planet Earth.

experiences.name: New York Walking Tours
experiences.description: Looking up at the towering skyscrapers and watching the rushing cars pairs perfectly with a walking tour. It's one thing to learn from the comfort of a museum, but it's another thing to walk within your object of study. With this selection of New York Walking Tours, you can get to know the city like never before.

experiences.name: Washington DC Day Trips from New York
experiences.description:
Explore the vibrant heart of the nation with an exhilarating day trip from the hustle and bustle of New York to the majestic streets of Washington DC. 
You'll be astounded by the blend of historic allure and landmarks in Washington DC.

experiences.name: Wollman Rink
experiences.description: Located in the heart of Manhattan's iconic Central Park, Wollman Rink is a picturesque ice skating haven. The park's most renowned rink is open from late October to early April. Experience the magic of ice skating for yourself as you glide and twirl past the beautiful scenery in Central Park.

experiences.name: EastVille Comedy Club
experiences.description:
EastVille is Brooklyn's only bonafide comedy club, and best of all, there's no drink minimum. That's no joke. 
EastVille's been running since 2008 and plays host to up-and-coming talent, along with established comedians. The fully-stocked bar also has everything it takes to create speciality cocktails. If you're looking for laughs, then you're in the right place.

experiences.name: Everson Museum of Art
experiences.description:
The Everson Museum of Art is for everyone, a place where community connects and inspiration surrounds you.
The Everson is a museum of firsts. It was the first museum to dedicate itself to the collection of American art, to create a permanent collection of ceramics, to collect video art, to create a docent program and to hire the now internationally-known architect I.M. Pei to design its building, a sculptural work of art in its own right. The Everson is home to over 10,000 works of art: American paintings, sculpture, drawings, video, graphics and one of the largest holdings of international ceramics in the nation.

experiences.name: New York Food Tours
experiences.description:
Discover the culinary delights of New York with a food tour! 
On any one of these New York food tours, you'll experience the best cuisine the Big Apple has to offer – from classic dishes to unique local specialties. 
Sample delicious food from some of the top restaurants in the city, and learn about the diverse culture and and fascinating history of New York's gastronomy.

experiences.name: Boston Day Trips from New York
experiences.description:
Get ready to experience the rich history and vibrant culture that Boston has to offer with a day trip from New York. 
With hassle-free transportation, the wonders of Boston are at your fingertips, promising a unique getaway.

experiences.name: Niagara Falls Day Trips from New York
experiences.description:
Experience unmatched natural beauty with a day trip from New York to the breathtaking Niagara Falls. 
Whether you want to enjoy the surroundings by yourself, on a guided tour, or with family and friends, a day at Niagara Falls is a day well spent.

experiences.name: The Banksy Museum
experiences.description: The Banksy Museum in NYC is home to the world’s largest collection of Banksy’s famous artwork. Celebrate the legacy of one of the greatest street artists of our time in this immersive exhibition, complete with life-size recreations of his iconic murals, a selection of his studio work, and other visual surprises.

experiences.name: Times Square
experiences.description: Times Square is one of the most iconic places in the United States, and arguably the world. Known for its bright neon lights and huge digital billboards as well as its famous New Year's Eve celebration, the square is close to several of New York's biggest tourist attractions like the Rockefeller Center, Gershwin Theatre, and the Museum of Modern Art.

experiences.name: Central Park New York
experiences.description: Looking for fun things to do in Central Park? Explore the city's iconic green space with our comprehensive guide to tours, activities, and more. Take a guided walking or bike tour, visit the Central Park Zoo, rent a boat on the lake, have a picnic, attend a concert, and much more. Whether you're a local or a first-time visitor, there's something for everyone in Central Park. Plan your adventure today!

experiences.name: Museum at Eldridge Street
experiences.description: The Museum at Eldridge Street is housed inside the breathtaking 1887 Eldridge Street Synagogue, a restored National Historic Landmark in Chinatown. The Museum at Eldridge Street is easy to reach on foot by walking through Chinatown.

experiences.name: New Amsterdam Theatre
experiences.description: The New Amsterdam Theatre is a state-of-the-art theater in Midtown Manhattan, at 214 West 42nd. This long-standing fixture of New York's theater scene first opened in 1903.

experiences.name: Stephen Sondheim Theatre
experiences.description:
In 1998, Roundabout renovated, reopened and operated the former Henry Miller’s Theatre as a Broadway home for their Tony award–winning production of Cabaret.﻿
However, nine months into the run, a construction accident at an adjacent building forced the theatre to close, and Cabaret transferred to Studio 54, where it ran for five more years.
In 2009, the Henry Miller’s Theatre reopened as the first LEED-certified Broadway theatre, raising the bar for environmentally sustainable design and construction of performing arts venues. In 2010, it was renamed the Stephen Sondheim in honor of the legendary composer and lyricist’s 80th birthday.

experiences.name: Ambassador Theatre
experiences.description: The Ambassador Theatre is a state-of-the-art theatre in New York. It's currently home to the award-winning musical hit Chicago.

experiences.name: Al Hirschfeld Theatre
experiences.description: The Al Hirschfeld Theatre is located at 302 West 45th Street, between 8th and 9th Avenue in New York City, USA. It's currently home to the musical spectacular Moulin Rouge, which features classic pop songs from across several decades.

experiences.name: Grand Central Terminal
experiences.description: Grand Central Terminal is an intercity railroad terminal in Midtown Manhattan, New York City, United States. The station's ornate architecture and striking facade make it a massive tourist attraction, drawing over 20 million visitors a year (aside from regular commuters). Grand Central Terminal is also the largest railway terminal in the world with 44 platforms.

experiences.name: Downtown Manhattan Heliport
experiences.description: The Downtown Manhattan Heliport is also called the Downtown Manhattan/Wall St. Heliport. This is the starting point for many helicopter flights around New York, and is located at Pier 6 on the East River in Manhattan, New York.

experiences.name: New York Stock Exchange
experiences.description: The New York Stock Exchange is an American stock exchange and financial zone. It is located on Wall Street, Lower Manhattan, New York City.

experiences.name: Minskoff Theatre
experiences.description: The Minskoff Theatre is a theatre on Broadway, New York, which has hosted shows including the Tony Award-winning The Lion King.

experiences.name: Gershwin Theatre
experiences.description:
The Gershwin Theatre is a Manhattan staple and has hosted many popular productions since it opened its doors in 1972. The theatre is named after brothers, George Gershwin, a composer, and Ira Gershwin, a lyricist. It is now home to the hit musical, Wicked. 
It is located at 222 West 51st Street in midtown-Manhattan, in the Paramount Plaza building.

experiences.name: Neil Simon Theatre
experiences.description: The Neil Simon Theatre is a Broadway theatre venue built in 1927 and located at 250 West 52nd Street in the Theater District of Midtown Manhattan, New York City. Formerly known as the Alvin Theatre, it has seating capacity for approximately 1,362 people.

experiences.name: Bryant Park
experiences.description: Bryant Park is a small park in Midtown Manhattan. Centrally located next to the New York Public Library, the park is a relaxed, leafy hang-out in NYC, housing a bar as well as hosting regularly organized events.

experiences.name: Eugene O'Neill Theatre
experiences.description:
The Eugene O'Neill Theatre is an iconic Broadway theatre venue located at 230 West 49th Street in midtown Manhattan.
Named after playwright Eugene O’Neill, the theatre can accommodate an audience of up to 1,108 and has been home to hit musicals like Big River: The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn, Spring Awakening, and the record-smashing, 2011 Tony Award Best Musical winner, The Book of Mormon – which is still running to this day.

experiences.name: 9/11 Museum Workshop
experiences.description:
The 9/11 Museum Workshop is centered around the award-winning photography of Gary Marlon Suson, the FDNY's official photographer during the search for the missing at Ground Zero after the September 11 attacks in New York City. 
This small but poignant museum allows visitors to see over 100 unique photos, as well as artifacts found at the dig site. Self-guided tours are accompanied by a multi-language audio guide.
Please note, it is located in the Meatpacking District on west 14th street (not at the World Trade Center site). Situated nearby are the High Line Park, Chelsea Market Eateries and the Whitney Museum. It is also just a few blocks from The Edge.

experiences.name: Society of Illustrators
experiences.description:
Founded in 1901, the Society of Illustrators is the oldest nonprofit organization dedicated to the art of illustration in the United States. 
Open since 1981, the Society of Illustrators Museum provides a unique opportunity to view current and historical works of art by notable illustrators and comic artists. Hosting various exhibitions and events, the museum is hub for creativity in NYC, hosting regular competitions, lectures, and workshops. It's located on E 63rd Street, New York.

experiences.name: Richard Rodgers Theatre
experiences.description:
This Richard Rodgers Theatre (named in honor of the legendary composer whose shows defined Broadway for over 30 years) has always been a house of hits, hosting a long line of famed musicals including: Anything Goes, Guys and Dolls, Damn Yankees, How to Succeed in Business Without Really Trying, The Best Little Whorehouse in Texas, Nine, Chicago, and Movin’ Out.
Since 2015, the Tony Winner for Best Musical and international smash hit Hamilton has called the Richard Rodgers Theatre home.

experiences.name: Brooks Atkinson Theatre
experiences.description:
The Brooks Atkinson Theatre has played host to dramas and comedies starring the likes of Matthew Broderick, Glenn Close, Richard Dreyfuss, Gene Hackman, Ed Harris, Derek Jacobi, Nathan Lane, Gary Sinise, Jerry Stiller, and Marisa Tomei.
If you're looking for your next Broadway Show, then you can guarantee quality here.

experiences.name: American Dream Mall
experiences.description: Shop at numerous retail stores and find a variety of products. Enjoy the indoor amusement park and have fun on various rides.

experiences.name: Walter Kerr Theatre
experiences.description:
In 1921, the Walter Kerr Theatre house opened under its original name, The Ritz. Owned by the Shuberts and designed by Herbert J. Krapp, it was intended to be a sister theater to the Ambassador Theatre.
Starting in 1939, CBS and ABC used the space to record radio and TV programs. In the 1970s it was converted back to a movie theater but was restored to legitimacy in 1983. Its current owners, Jujamcyn Theatres, spent millions restoring the theater in 1990 and it has housed successful Broadway runs ever since.

experiences.name: The High Line
experiences.description:
The High Line is a prime example of how to transform an urban landscape into an innovative green space. This former New York Central Railroad line has been converted into a "living system" that features landscape architecture, urban design, and ecology. 
Highlights of the High Line include views of the Hudson and the city from the Meatpacking District to the northern edge of the West Side Yard on 34th Street, as well as an urban garden setting with hundreds of species of plants.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Public Transport Tickets
experiences.description:
Experience the beauty of Amsterdam with the ease of public transport. 
With the city's comprehensive network of trams, metros, and buses, you can explore Amsterdam from every angle. By securing your tickets in advance, you can get to the city's hot spots spots quickly and safely.
Don't worry about having to find the right ticket at the station, or on the tram; make your time in the Dutch capital as easy as possible by picking up your Amsterdam Public transport tickets ahead of time.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Canal Cruises
experiences.description:
The best way to enjoy Amsterdam is through its most unique feature – the canals. Choose from an array of Amsterdam canal cruises and soak up the city's history, architecture, and fascinating views!
Amsterdam boat tours offer a range of experiences for guests of all ages and interests. Choose from a morning canal cruise served with breakfast, or a leisurely day tour with your choice of pizza, BBQ, or Gouda cheese tasting. Let the captain do the steering while you enjoy a drink (or two) on a booze cruise, or book a romantic evening excursion instead. There are plenty of options!
Get to know Amsterdam with an expert guide to point out historic points of interest, showcase classic photography opportunities, and share fun facts about the city. Canal tours are available in several languages and most offer audio guides. Rain or shine, these semi-covered tour boats are your ticket to experience this iconic city in true Dutch fashion.

experiences.name: Embassy of the Free Mind
experiences.description: Explore a collection of ancient manuscripts and rare books. Attend lectures and discussions on philosophy and spirituality.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Schiphol Airport Transfers
experiences.description: Enjoy easer transfers between Amsterdam Schiphol Airport and locations in and around Amsterdam.

experiences.name: Van Gogh Museum
experiences.description:
The Van Gogh Museum displays over 200 of Vincent van Gogh's works, including landscapes, still lifes and drawings, and letters. By displaying them alongside works from Van Gogh's contemporaries, viewers get real insights into his life and work.
The museum itself is comprised of two buildings. The main building, designed by Gerrit Rietveld, Joan van Dillen, and J. van Tricht, opened in 1973. The second building, designed by Japanese architect Kisho Kurokawa was added in 1999. Located in Amsterdam's Museumplein (along with the Stedelijk and Rijksmuseum), the Van Gogh Museum is one of the most-visited museums in the Netherlands.
Book Van Gogh Museum tickets and get up close to masterpieces like "The Potato Eaters," "Almond Blossom," "Two Cut Sunflowers", and "Irises," not to mention one or two of those timelessly intense self portraits!

experiences.name: Moco Museum Amsterdam
experiences.description:
The Modern Contemporary (Moco) Museum Amsterdam is housed inside Villa Alsberg, a 1904 townhouse in the middle of Amsterdam Museumplein. 
High-profile guest curators and exhibitions highlight some of the modern world's best contributors to the art world. Eduard Cuypers, cousin of Rijksmuseum designer Pierre Cuypers, designed the townhouse, making it a sight to see in itself. 
But the work inside is absolutely phenomenal; two current attractions showcase the work of street art activist Banksy, and pop artist extraordinaire, Andy Warhol.

experiences.name: Madame Tussauds Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Madame Tussauds is located in the center of Amsterdam at the ever-busy Dam Square. Founded by wax sculptor Marie Tussaud in the 18th century, this iconic wax museum chain has locations in many cities across the globe, including London, Berlin, New York, Las Vegas, Hong Kong, and Shanghai, and of course, the Dutch capital. 
Madame Tussauds Amsterdam is home to hundreds of historical and royal figures, film and sports stars, and immersive themed galleries. The museum encourages an interactive approach to the wax figures – so go ahead and hug your favorite celebrity crush! Madame Tussauds has been a central fixture in Amsterdam since 1971, with its first location at the Kalverstraat, before being moved to the Dam Square, where it can't be missed – and definitely shouldn't!

experiences.name: Efteling
experiences.description:
Originally opened in 1952 as a fairytale forest with animatronic characters, Efteling has grown in size and stature to become one of Europe's premier themeparks. 
With rides, restaurants, and attractions, visitors will be immersed in a magical forest full of elves and fairies from Dutch folklore.

experiences.name: Amsterdam City Tours
experiences.description:
Explore the beauty of Amsterdam on a city tour! Discover the city's vibrant culture, architecture, and history while taking in the sights and sounds of the Dutch capital. 
With knowledgeable and experienced tour guides, you are sure to get the most out of your Amsterdam experience. Get ready to discover the historic canal ring with its quaint bridges, as well as hidden gems you could easily walk past.
There's also a range of self-guided audio tour option so you can enjoy Amsterdam at your own pace.

experiences.name: THIS IS HOLLAND
experiences.description:
THIS IS HOLLAND offers a simulated flight experience with a spherical screen and special effects to enhance a state-of-the-art journey over The Netherlands. Visitors will see cultural highlights and landmarks, as well as learn about the history of The Netherlands.
THIS IS HOLLAND is located in Amsterdam and can be reached via ferry from Amsterdam Central Station.

experiences.name: ARTIS Amsterdam Royal Zoo
experiences.description:
ARTIS Amsterdam Royal Zoo is a famous zoo and botanical garden founded in 1838. It is the Netherlands' oldest zoo and one of the oldest in the world, featuring several historic buildings and architectural gems that provide a dose of Dutch culture alongside incredible wildlife encounters.
Located in the heart of the city, ARTIS Amsterdam Royal Zoo covers an area of over 10 hectares and is home to some 700 species of animals, including Asian elephants, African penguins, net giraffes, jaguars, and more. The zoo is also renowned for its botanical gardens containing some of the world's rarest trees and exotic plants.
The zoo has a variety of interactive exhibitions, including a Planetarium, Aquarium, Geological Museum, Insectarium, and a Butterfly Pavilion. There are also many educational and recreational activities for kids, including a petting zoo, playgrounds, and an interactive science center.

experiences.name: Fabrique des Lumières
experiences.description:
See art come to life at Fabrique des Lumières - the largest digital art center in the Netherlands - in the Westergasterrein, Amsterdam.
A large industrial space with walls up to 17 meters high features immersive exhibitions by classic, modern and contemporary artists. With advanced technology, the artworks are brought to life by light projections and accompanying music, as well as a downloadable app that will further enrich your experience.

experiences.name: Heineken Experience
experiences.description:
The Heineken Experience is located in the original Heineken Brewery. Gerard Adriaan Heineken bought this building back in 1863 when it was the De Hooiberg brewery. He then turned it towards the production of his eponymous beer. The building remained a brewery all the way up to 1988. It was then that it became a museum (and hotspot for Heineken consumption).
Located in the vibrant neighborhood of De Pijp, the Heineken Experience is ideally situated for a canal cruise, and some city exploration that takes you away from the hustle and bustle of Dam Square.
Since 2001, it's been the world-famous Heineken Experience. After a year-long update and redesign in 2008, the Heineken Experience is more Heineken-y and Experience-y than ever before.

experiences.name: NEMO Science Museum
experiences.description: NEMO Science Museum is a science centre in Amsterdam, located at the Oosterdok. It was built on top of the IJ-tunnel that connects the centre of Amsterdam with the north of the city. The striking building, designed by Italian architect and engineer, Renzo Piano, opened its doors in 1997. Some say the shape of the building mimics the appearance of a large boat, but others suggest that it's actually a reflection of the tunnel it was built upon.

experiences.name: The Upside Down
experiences.description:
The Upside Down is the biggest Instagram museum in Europe – but it's more than just a museum. It's part amusement park, part club, part magic mushroom trip! Get your friends together and remember to charge your phones; you'll need battery power for all the photos you'll take in this mind-melting world of fun. Explore the boundaries of your own creativity, trick your brain and tickle your senses.
The Upside Down is located at Europaboulevard in Amsterdam, just a short tram ride or cycle from central Amsterdam.

experiences.name: Rembrandt House Museum
experiences.description: View original artworks and personal belongings of Rembrandt. Watch demonstrations of traditional etching and painting techniques.

experiences.name: Keukenhof Day Trip
experiences.description:
Discover the beauty of the Netherlands and take a day trip to Keukenhof. You'll be amazed at the colorful and fragrant tulip fields that span the landscape. 
Explore the picturesque paths of the park and take in the unique Dutch culture. With a variety of activities and plenty of photo opportunities, you're sure to have a perfect day in the Dutch countryside.

experiences.name: Stedelijk Museum Amsterdam
experiences.description:
The Stedelijk Museum in Amsterdam was founded in 1874 and officially opened its doors in 1895. The building was designed by the architect Adriaan Willem Weissman and is located at Museum Square, where you can also find The Rijksmuseum and The Van Gogh Museum.
From 2004 until 2012 The Stedelijk Museum was undergoing a renovation. The building was extended with an extra wing, this wing is also known as 'The Bathtub.'

experiences.name: Pancake Boat Amsterdam
