experiences.description: The Pancake Boat, or Pannenkoekenboot, is a buffet experience in Amsterdam where guests can eat delicious Dutch pancakes while cruising the IJ. The Pancake Boat docks at Amsterdam NDSM, a brief ferry ride away from Centraal Station.

experiences.name: The Amsterdam Dungeon
experiences.description:
The Amsterdam Dungeon first opened its doors to the public of Amsterdam in 2006 and has been scaring people ever since. The attraction is located inside an historical church and neo-gothic elements of the architecture date back to 1912. You can even see them in some scenes of the show!
The Dungeon is located right in central Amsterdam and can be reached easily on foot or by bike.
The Amsterdam Dungeon is a spooktacular journey into the darkside of the city's 500-year-old history. It's right in the city center.

experiences.name: Self-Guided Tours Amsterdam
experiences.description: Discover the enchanting city of Amsterdam at your own pace with Self-Guided Tours Amsterdam. Immerse yourself in the rich history, artistic heritage, and vibrant culture of this captivating city. Our self-guided tours provide the freedom to explore Amsterdam's iconic landmarks, charming neighborhoods, and world-class museums. Wander along the picturesque canals, marvel at the masterpieces in the Van Gogh Museum, and soak up the lively atmosphere of Dam Square. With Self-Guided Tours Amsterdam, create your personalized itinerary, follow our expert guides, and uncover the hidden gems that make Amsterdam truly special. Start your self-guided adventure and experience the magic of Amsterdam.

experiences.name: Amsterdam City Cards
experiences.description:
Experience all the best of Amsterdam with a city card! Save time and money on your sightseeing adventures with an all-in-one pass that gives you access to many of the city's top attractions. 
Discover all the city has to offer with range of convenient and budget-friendly city card. 
Explore the city's canals and historical hotspots, as well as its unmissable museums. With a city card, you're guaranteed to make the most of your time in Amsterdam. Get your city card today and experience what makes Amsterdam so special!

experiences.name: Royal Palace Amsterdam (Koninklijk Paleis)
experiences.description:
The impressive Royal Palace Amsterdam, located on Dam Square, is one of Amsterdam's top attractions. It was built in the Dutch Golden Age as the city hall for Amsterdam. It later became a palace for the royal family, a function it still has to this day. Consequently, it's not always open to the public.
It plays a key role during State visits and it's also home to some pretty amazing 17th-century art - representing the Dutch Golden Age.

experiences.name: Red Light Secrets Museum
experiences.description: The Red Light Secrets Museum, or Amsterdam's Museum of Prostitution, is a window into what goes on in the famous (or notorious?) Red Light District. Located at Oudezijds Achterburgwal 60H, the museum is just a 5-minute walk from Centraal Station, on one of the picturesque canals that make up the area the locals call 'de Wallen'.

experiences.name: The Cat Cabinet
experiences.description:
The Cat Cabinet (Het KattenKabinet) is puuurrfectly located in the 'Golden Bend' of the Herengracht. Situated on the classically-furnished piano nobile of a storied 17th-century canal house, there are many rooms full of cat paraphernalia (and real cats). 
This house (and the one next to it) were once the property of the Van Eeghen family. In 1985 it was bought by the Stichting Herengracht 497 and restored, eventually becoming the feline-filled fantasy it is today.
It's an easy tram or bike ride from most places in Amsterdam.

experiences.name: WONDR Experience
experiences.description: WONDR Experience is an immersive experience for adults and kids alike (but mostly adults!) that stimulates the senses with candy-colored dive-in pools filled with marshmallows and more. It is located in Amsterdam North, less than 10 minutes away from Amsterdam Central Station.

experiences.name: ARTIS Micropia
experiences.description: Micropia is a museum dedicated to the microscopic life of microbes. It is located beside Amsterdam's ARTIS Zoo. It is located east of the Amstel and is easily accessible by tram, or ten minutes' walk from Waterlooplein metro station.

experiences.name: Rijksmuseum
experiences.description:
The National Museum of the Netherlands, or the Rijksmuseum, has been housed on this location since 1885. It recently underwent a ten-year, €375 million renovation. The Rijksmuseum has over 5,000 paintings (including Rembrandt's Night Watch) and has an emphasis on the Dutch Golden Age. But it's not just paintings – there are sculptures, antiques, collections of clothing and weapons, and one of the finest actively-used libraries you'll ever see. 
The Dutch architect P.J.H. Cuypers designed this elegant building, and even put himself in the design as one of the statues on the roof.

experiences.name: Playhood
experiences.description: Join a fun experience at 'The Hood.' This place is great for both kids and adults, where you can pretend to be a fireman, pizza chef, or astronaut. You can also get your play gear at Albert Klein. Each session lasts 1.5 hours, giving you access to the play area and café. You can try specialty coffees and Dutch toasties.

experiences.name: Dutch Resistance Museum
experiences.description: The Dutch Resistance Museum explores the period from 14 May 1940 to 5 May 1945, when the Netherlands was occupied by Nazi Germany. Visitors can learn about the Dutch way of life via a series of exhibitions featuring artifacts, objects, video displays, and more.

experiences.name: Foam Photography Museum Amsterdam
experiences.description: This building on the Keizersgracht was originally built in 1685. In the 19th century it was already in use as a museum. First by local Amsterdam artists, it's currently Amsterdam's renowned Photography Museum.

experiences.name: Dutch Pancake Masters
experiences.description:
Get to enjoy a variety of classic Dutch pancakes at Dutch Pancake Masters. 
Choose from sweet or savory options, with toppings like fresh berries, crispy bacon, and syrup. A great spot for breakfast, lunch, or a snack. Relax in a cozy setting while you taste the Dutch tradition.

experiences.name: Hortus Botanicus
experiences.description: Walk through different themed gardens and observe unique plant species. Enjoy the peaceful atmosphere and attend educational workshops.

experiences.name: Wereldmuseum Amsterdam
experiences.description:
The beautiful and historic building in which the Wereldmuseum Amsterdam is housed is a little out of the main tourist areas. It's in leafy Amsterdam Oost, not far from the Artis Zoo. 
Formerly the Tropenmuseum, this museum is dedicated to other cultures and countries, and is one of Amsterdam's hidden treasures.

experiences.name: Museum Our Lord in the Attic
experiences.description:
This 17th-century canal house has a literal highlight - a miniature, yet fully functional, church hidden in its attic!
Now Amsterdam's oldest museum, Ons' Lieve Heer Op Solder is a beautiful slice of perfectly preserved history and exemplifies the city's tolerant attitude towards culture and religion.

experiences.name: Nationaal Holocaustmuseum
experiences.description:
Once a thriving theatre before World War II, the Hollandsche Schouwburg tragically became a Nazi collection point for the deportation of Jews from July 1942 onward. 
Thousands were detained here for hours, days, or even weeks before being dispatched to concentration and extermination camps. Today, the former theatre stands as a poignant memorial, honoring all victims of the Holocaust.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Light Festival Cruises
experiences.description:
The Amsterdam Light Festival transforms the city's iconic canals into a sparkling winter playground, filled with illuminated artworks from some of the world's top artists. 
Of course, the best way to experience the dazzling artistry is from the water. This selection of cruises lets you get up close and personal with the best festival from the UNESCO-protected canal network.

experiences.name: A'DAM Lookout
experiences.description:
A'DAM Lookout sits atop A’DAM Toren, the newly refurbished 22-story 'vertical city,' on the north side of the IJ. It offers an unimpeded 360-degree view of Amsterdam from almost 100 meters above ground, on the city’s only publicly accessible observation deck.
The A’DAM Toren is one of the highlights of the transformation of the IJ riverbank.

experiences.name: Highlights of Holland
experiences.description: Highlights of Holland is an interactive museum that introduces visitors to the Netherlands in just over an hour. Move through ten themed rooms that showcase the country’s art, landscapes, and traditions.

experiences.name: Barbie - The Dream Experience
experiences.description: Explore life-sized versions of iconic Barbie toys and famous scenes. Attend interactive exhibits and take photos with iconic dolls.

experiences.name: BODY WORLDS - The Happiness Project
experiences.description:
The German anatomist Gunther von Hagens, who invented the plastination technique in the late 1970s at teh University of Heidelberg, developed this exhibition.
The Body Worlds Exhibition was first presented in 1995. Showing whole bodies plastinated in lifelike poses and dissected to show various structures and systems of human anatomy, which has since met with public interest and controversy in more than 50 museums and venues around the world.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Winter Paradise
experiences.description:
Check out one of the best Amsterdam winter activities at Winter Paradise, hosted at RAI Amsterdam. 
The convention center turns into a wintery wonderland with a festive Ferris wheel, warm mulled wine, and ice skating. 
A frosty festival awaits after dusk including drinks, dancing, and live music.

experiences.name: XtraCold Icebar Amsterdam
experiences.description: Welcome to the coolest bar in Amsterdam (literally). This teeth-chattering locale is the perfect spot to sample the lifestyle of a Polar explorer, without the risk of frostbite. Just meters from the busy Rembrandt Square, this is a great stop on a night on the town in Amsterdam. Free drinks and thermals should keep you warm. The bar is sculpted from 60 tons of natural ice and even your glass here is made from frozen H2O!

experiences.name: Zaanse Schans & Volendam Day Trips from Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Escape the hustle-bustle of Amsterdam for a day and take a scenic trip to the quaint towns of Zaanse Schans and Volendam. 
Get the complete Dutch experience with a visit to the historic windmills located in Zaanse Schans and the delicious cheese factories which offer free tastings!
Also visit the old Dutch town of Volendam, with its colorful wooden houses and fishing boats in the harbor, your day is bound to be exciting.

experiences.name: Giethoorn Daytrips from Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Explore the stunning Dutch village of Giethoorn on a day trip from Amsterdam! 
Discover the unique beauty of this traditional village, where canals and waterways take the place of roads. Enjoy a relaxing boat tour, explore the charming streets and take in the breathtaking natural surroundings.

experiences.name: Amaze Amsterdam
experiences.description:
AMAZE Amsterdam is an immersive experience created by the world's largest dance event organizer ID&T. Visitors experience a journey from head to heart, where they are taken through seven experience worlds filled with state-of-the-art audiovisual technology.
A labyrinth of storytelling is on offer, with shows full of lasers, lights, visuals, 11D sound, interactive effects and especially sound and music. AMAZE combines 30 years of show experience into a new, exciting way of going out – the next phase in the evolution of entertainment.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Hop-on Hop-off Tours
experiences.description:
Experience Amsterdam in the most convenient way possible with a Hop-on Hop-off tour of the city! 
These experiences allow you to explore Amsterdam with ease – simply hop on one of the many buses, or boats, and explore the city at your own pace. 
Take the stress out of your trip to Amsterdam and enjoy quick and easy transportation to the city's main sights. Each Hop-on Hop-off tour includes a sightseeing route with plenty of information, as well as convenient stops at top attractions where you can hop off!

experiences.name: Nxt Museum
experiences.description: Nxt Museum is the home of new media art in the Netherlands, located in Amsterdam North. Nxt Museum specializes in large-scale, multi-sensory installations fusing technology and artistic expression.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Tulip Museum
experiences.description: Learn about the history of tulips and their impact on Dutch culture. View a variety of tulip-related artifacts and exhibits.

experiences.name: Museum Het Schip
experiences.description: Museum Het Schip is located in a social housing complex called The Ship, and is one of the most famous and iconic buildings of the Amsterdam School. It is easy to reach via public transport from all areas of Amsterdam.

experiences.name: The New Church Amsterdam
experiences.description: Located in Amsterdam's Dam Square, De Nieuwe Kerk is a historic monument known for its exhibitions of art, photography, and diverse cultures. With 250,000 visitors annually, it's a popular cultural hub hosting royal ceremonies and cultural events, embodying Dutch culture and history.

experiences.name: Muiderslot Castle
experiences.description: Muiderslot Castle is a defensive castle built in the middle ages, just outside of Amsterdam, North Holland. It is now a museum housing relics of the medieval era, as well as its famous residents like Pieter Corneliszoon Hooft. It is roughly 20 kilometers south east of Amsterdam and can be reached via public transport, or bike, in roughly an hour.

experiences.name: Museum of The Canals
experiences.description:
In a monumental (and gorgeous!) building on the Herengracht, lies the Museum of the Canals (Grachtenmuseum). In this museum you'll develop a deeper appreciation for the Dutch capital - and the waterways that are such an important part of this city's identity. 
This museum uses a broad set of tools - holograms, paintings, models, audio guides, and architecture (among others) to bring the significance and experience of Amsterdam's famous canals to life.

experiences.name: Red Light District Amsterdam Tour
experiences.description:
Explore Amsterdam's notorious Red Light District on a number of available tours. 
Whether with a guide, or a self-guided visit, you'll learn all about the history and cultural significance of Amsterdam's Red Light District.

experiences.name: Aloha Amsterdam
experiences.description: Aloha is a Hawaiian-themed entertainment complex in Amsterdam featuring a full bowling alley, a glow-in-the-dark minigolf course, a laser tag arena, and a grill restaurant and bar. It's the ideal spot for friends and families looking to enjoy a bit of old-fashioned fun in the Dutch capital.

experiences.name: Jewish Cultural Quarter
experiences.description:
The Jewish Cultural Quarter includes the Jewish Museum (formerly known as the Jewish Historical Museum), the Portuguese Synagogue (built in 1675), the Hollandsche Schouwburg (National Holocaust Memorial), and the Jewish Museum Junior (an educational children's museum designed like a traditional Jewish home).
The Jewish Museum itself is housed in four monumental synagogues near Waterlooplein, making it a great place to start on a family day out in central Amsterdam.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Food Tours
experiences.description:
Explore Amsterdam's delicious side through a food tour of the city! Sample traditional Dutch snacks, visit the best markets, and delve into the city's culinary heritage. 
Learn about the unique flavors, recipes, and local ingredients that make Amsterdam's food scene so special. Prepare to bite into bitterballen, savour the sweet taste of stroopwafels, and find out if you can handle raw herring. 
If you have a sweet tooth, then don't miss out on a pancake cooking class.

experiences.name: Banksy Museum Amsterdam
experiences.description: View a wide range of street art and graffiti. Attend exhibitions showcasing works by different artists.

experiences.name: Anne Frank Tours
experiences.description:
Pass by the house by the Westerkerk where Anne Frank and her family including four other people, evaded the Nazis by hiding in secret rooms at the back of this quaint 17th century canal house. 
It is a moving testament to the resilience of the human spirit, and a melancholy memorial to the young girl who, through her writing, put a very personal and innocent face on the horror of the Holocaust.

experiences.name: Eye Film Museum
experiences.description: Eye Filmmuseum is the museum for film and the art of the moving image. Located in the iconic building on the IJ River, Eye organizes exhibitions at the intersection of film and visual arts and film programs that shed light on the very earliest film history, to the most current social developments. Eye's collection includes more than 50,000 Dutch and international films and a multitude of posters, photographs, film equipment, books and personal archives, including the renowned Unesco World Heritage Collection of film pioneer Jean Desmet. Eye preserves, restores and presents film; from classics, blockbusters and early avant-garde films to the very latest VR experiments.

experiences.name: Van Gogh & Rembrandt in Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Explore the captivating "Van Gogh & Rembrandt in Amsterdam" show, where the works of these two master artists are showcased in an immersive sound and light show inside the historic Noorderkerk.
Learn how Vincent van Gogh was inspired by Rembrandt during his time in Amsterdam. Get closer to the art and see how Rembrandt's work guided Van Gogh's pieces. Feel the narrative brought to life through readings of Van Gogh's letters to his brother as you watch the church walls transform into a 360° canvas.

experiences.name: Ripley's Believe It or Not! Amsterdam
experiences.description: Located on Amsterdam’s Dam Square, this cabinet of curiosities showcases more than 500 exhibits and artifacts in its five floors. The items, ranging from actual Olympic torches to a very unusual Fabergé egg, are organized in 19 themed galleries. There’s also a spacious café with a view to the square and a 5D cinema on the fourth floor.

experiences.name: House of Bols
experiences.description:
The House of Bols is a stylish combo of the brand new and the very old. Genever is the Dutch spirit from which gin evolved, and here you’ll find out everything about Lucas Bols – the world’s oldest distilled spirit brand and their 38 liqueurs.
It will also introduce you to the more modern world of cocktail making, and the flair is supported by the knowledge that it’s a recent winner of the Dutch Design Award for Best Exhibition and Experience.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Boat Rental
experiences.description:
Explore Amsterdam in a unique way and rent a boat to cruise around the canals! 
Take in the beauty of the Dutch capital from a fresh perspective, and make sure to bring your camera for the perfect photo opportunity. 
Enjoy the convenience of a self-guided tour and discover the historic and modern architecture, the vibrant culture, and the bustling activity of Amsterdam by renting a boat. With an easy booking process and flexible rentals, you can create a memorable experience in no time!

experiences.name: Lisse Drive-It-Yourself Tours
experiences.description:
Welcome to Lisse Drive-It-Yourself Tours, where the open road becomes your playground. Experience the ultimate freedom of exploring your destination at your own pace, embracing the thrill of spontaneity and uncovering hidden gems along the way. With our self-guided tours, you have the power to create unforgettable memories and embark on unique adventures. 
Whether you crave the scenic countryside or the vibrant city streets, our diverse range of destinations caters to all preferences. From charming villages to iconic landmarks, the choice is yours. Book your Lisse Drive-It-Yourself Tour today and unleash the explorer within you.

experiences.name: Museumplein Ice Rink
experiences.description: Skate on the large ice rink with friends and family. Enjoy warm drinks at nearby cafes after a fun session on the ice.

experiences.name: Zaanse Schans Tours
experiences.description: Situated on the picturesque Zaan River, Zaanse Schans is an open-air museum that features six working windmills, historic houses, and craft shops. Amble about its quaint lanes and you'll find yourself understanding just how the Dutch lived (and prospered!) in bygone centuries. All of that as you see windmills hard at work and clogs being made.

experiences.name: Boom Chicago
experiences.description:
Located a clog's throw from the center of Amsterdam, Boom Chicago is arguably the holy grail of improv comedy in mainland Europe. 
Founded by three Americans in 1993, this international creative comedy group has launched the careers of comedic superstars like Seth Meyers, Jordan Peele, Amber Ruffin, Kay Cannon (Pitch Perfect), Heather Anne Campbell (Rick & Morty), and the creators and stars of Ted Lasso (Jason Sudeikis, Brendan Hunt, and Joe Kelly).

experiences.name: H'ART Museum Hermitage Amsterdam
experiences.description: Visit the H'ART Museum in Amsterdam, a cultural hub with an impressive collection from antiquity to the present. Located in a historic landmark, it's a must-see destination. Enjoy the dynamic atmosphere, indoor garden, and lively Grand Café offering various activities like concerts, lectures, movie nights, and art education programs. The museum shares its space with the Amsterdam Museum and Museum of the Mind.

experiences.name: Hash Marihuana & Hemp Museum
experiences.description: The world's oldest cannabis museum can be found right in the heart of Amsterdam's Red Light District. Learn all about the history and uses of this popular and controversial plant.

experiences.name: Belgium Day Trips from Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Leave the charismatic Dutch canals behind and experience the vibrancy of Belgium, just a stone's throw away from Amsterdam! 
Picture gazing at exquisite architecture, savoring rich chocolates, and engaging with historical narratives woven into the fabric of Belgium's towns and cities. 
Every moment of these day trips teems with beauty and intrigue, promising a memorable adventure across the border!

experiences.name: Huis Willet-Holthuysen
experiences.description: This house on the Herengracht was built in 1685-87 for then-mayor Jacob Hop. Louisa Willet-Holthuysen, widow of art collector Abraham Willet (1828-1888), vacated the property in 1895, leaving the house and its art collection to the city of Amsterdam.

experiences.name: Bike Rentals Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Amsterdam is a popular destination for tourists, and many people enjoy exploring the city by bicycle. 
We work with multiple suppliers to offer a variety of different bikes available for rent, including both traditional handbrake and electric bikes. Amsterdam is known for its extensive network of bike paths and waterways, making it an ideal destination for a cycling adventure.

experiences.name: Rotterdam and The Hague Day Trips from Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Take a captivating day-long adventure to Rotterdam and The Hague from Amsterdam! 
Experience the thrilling pulse of Rotterdam's vibrant metropolitan life, then get swept away by the regal charm and grandeur of Dutch political heart, The Hague. 
This delightfully diverse day trip offers a fascinating peek into two entirely unique Dutch worlds.

experiences.name: Oude Kerk, Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Discover 700 years of Dutch history within the walls of Amsterdam's oldest building – the Oude Kerk (Old Church). 
The Oude Kerk Amsterdam now serves as the city's newest art institute with permanent and temporary exhibitions showcasing the history and culture of Amsterdam.

experiences.name: Dining Cruises Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Let your taste buds drift away along the canals of Amsterdam with any number of tasty dining cruises in the Dutch capital.
Lunch, dinner, drinks – you name it, and there's a way to wine and dine onboard at any time of day.

experiences.name: The Sloten Windmill
experiences.description:
The Sloten Windmill is a functioning draining mill. Known in Dutch as the Molen Van Sloten, it is also the only windmill in Amsterdam which is open to the public on a daily basis.
The Sloten Windmill is located on the outskirts of Amsterdam, not too far from Schiphol Airport. It is easily reached by car and public transport.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Parking
experiences.description:
Looking for parking in Amsterdam? Look no further! These options allow you to park with ease and peace of mind, so you can enjoy your time in the city without worrying about where to leave your car. 
These parking garages have been chosen for you based on their location, convenience, and safety, so your car will be in good hands.
Be sure to reserve your parking spot in advance to guarantee a spot and avoid any unnecessary stress.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Museum
experiences.description: The Amsterdam Museum tells the story of the Dutch capital, from the Middle Ages through the 17th century, and right up to modern times. When it opened its doors in 1926 it was located in the Waag at the Nieuwmarkt. In 1975 the museum moved to its current location, just off the Kalverstraat, a building that was originally a municipal orphange. The collection is full of photos, artifacts and interactive exhibits.

experiences.name: Henri Willig Store
experiences.description: Henri Willig's cheeses are a global phenomenon. The exclusive baby cheese with the colored packages are an essential part of many supermarkets. Goat cheese, organic cheese and all kinds cow cheeses combined with ingredients such as garlic and chili... Willig's cheeses are unique and you'll find them here!

experiences.name: Rembrandts Amsterdam Experience
experiences.description: Prepare to step back to the 17th century with Rembrandt Amsterdam Experience. This 5D experience uses music, video projections and special effects to transport you back to Amsterdam as it was when Rembrandt was a citizen of the city, showing a reconstruction of Rembrandt's studio on the Rozengracht canal where he created his final masterpieces.

experiences.name: Frida Kahlo Exhibition Amsterdam
experiences.description: View a collection of Frida Kahlo's iconic paintings. Learn about her life and artistic journey through detailed exhibits and multimedia displays.

experiences.name: Het Scheepvaartmuseum (National Maritime Museum)
experiences.description: Het Scheepvaartmuseum has a fascinating collection of historical nautical items, including a replica of the 18th-century cargo ship Amsterdam. The building was founded in 1656 as a storehouse for the Admiralty in Amsterdam.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Beer Experiences
experiences.description:
Get a true taste of Amsterdam with all kinds of beer experiences.
Your personal tour guide will take you to some of the best breweries in the city, giving you an insider's look at the Amsterdam beer scene.

experiences.name: Johan Cruijff ArenA
experiences.description:
Amsterdam's Ajax ArenA is so awesome that they spell it with a capital 'A' at the front and the back of the name. 
It's the largest stadium in the whole country and has a retractable roof. When not being used for Ajax (or national team) games, big ticket concerts are sometimes held here. Artists including Madonna, Michael Jackson, David Bowie, and The Rolling Stones have all played gigs here.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Bike Tours
experiences.description:
Explore Amsterdam like a local on a bike tour. Cycle your way around the city and get a first-hand understanding of why the Dutch have embraced the bike! 
These pedal-powered tours allow you to experience the real Amsterdam, from its beautiful canals to its bustling markets. With the help of an experienced guide, you'll get to see the city like never before. 
So, hop on a bike, enjoy some fresh air, and experience a tour that you won't soon forget.

experiences.name: Day Trips from Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Experience the beauty of the Netherlands, and beyond, with an amazing day trip from Amsterdam! 
Discover the Lowlands' stunning landscapes and charming villages as you journey through the Dutch countryside to your destination. 
There's plenty of day trips to choose from, including relaxing boat rides along the quaint canals of Giethoorn, to informative tours of locations like Volendam and Bruges.
Explore Dutch culture and learn about some of the country's most famous symbols, including windmills and tulip fields. 
Leave Amsterdam for the day and make memories that will last a lifetime.

experiences.name: Cannabis Museum Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Welcome to Cannabis Museum Amsterdam, your premier destination for an immersive and educational journey into the world of cannabis. Located in the vibrant heart of Amsterdam, we take pride in offering an adventurous and interactive experience like no other.
Explore the fascinating history of cannabis, from its diverse uses as an industrial product and superfood to its role as a valuable medicine. Delve into the captivating story of how humanity has domesticated, cultivated, and spread this remarkable plant from its origins on the Central Asian steppes to the iconic coffeeshops of Amsterdam and beyond. Join us for a hands-on exploration, where you’ll discover the rich history, unravel the scientific wonders, and dispel the misconceptions surrounding this extraordinary plant.

experiences.name: Diamond Museum
experiences.description:
The Diamond Museum is Amsterdam's sparkliest attraction, plunging you into the history of the city's diamond trade and showcasing world-famous diamonds from all over the globe.
It's in central Amsterdam and can be easily reached on foot or by bike.

experiences.name: Hard Rock Cafe Amsterdam
experiences.description: The Hard Rock Cafe Amsterdam is one of the most popular restaurants for visitors to the city. It offers good food and service in a top canal-side location, close to Leidseplein and Vondelpark.

experiences.name: The BonTon Tour
experiences.description: BonTon Stripclub Amsterdam is the oldest, biggest, and best-rated gentlemen's club that Amsterdam has to offer. It's located right in the heart of Amsterdam, just a 5-minute walk from Leidseplein.

experiences.name: Ctaste - Dining in the dark Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Want dinner with a difference? Ctaste has what you're looking for, but it might be hard to see. This unique dining experience takes place in total darkness; there isn't a light to be found!
Visually impaired waiters will take your order, serve your meal, and be there to take any extra drink orders. With one of your senses removed, your sense of taste will feel much more intense. So, focus on the textures, indulge in the aromas of your wine, and enjoy.

experiences.name: ARTIS Groote Museum
experiences.description:
The museum of big questions. From communicating plants to a competition with a chimpanzee, or a smell tunnel where recognition and emotion alternate. Expect a personal expedition as you criss-cross through the museum.
Built between 1850 and 1855 for the members of the Society Natura Artis Magistra, it stands on the oldest grounds of ARTIS and at the time was one of the most modern buildings in Amsterdam. The museum, which closed in 1947, has since been completely renovated and restored.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Photoshoots
experiences.description:
Make more than just memories with a photo shoot in beautiful Amsterdam. There's no better backdrop than the Dutch capital.
Whether you’re a tourist or a local, the city’s iconic canals, vibrant streets, and historic buildings make it the perfect location for any photo shoot. Join an experienced photographer and capture all the best moments of your trip!

experiences.name: Volendam Day Trips from Amsterdam
experiences.description:
Discover the beauty of the Dutch countryside and take a day trip to Volendam, a small fishing village near Amsterdam that's packed with charm. 
Admire the traditional Dutch architecture, experience traditions that are centuries old, and wander through the quaint streets. While you're visiting, make sure you sample the local cuisine and pick up some Dutch cheese, or maybe even a pair of clogs, to take home!
The culture and history of this unique and picturesque village is sure to fascinate you. You can also take a ride across the Markermeer lake and visit the idyllic former island of Marken. 
Enjoy a day of sightseeing and leisure, and make the most of your visit to the Netherlands!

experiences.name: Amsterdam Nightlife
experiences.description:
Amsterdam is one of Europe's most vibrant cities, and its nightlife is no exception! 
Known around the world for its DJs, it's no surprise that Amsterdam is full of quality clubs. There's also plenty of stylish bars and laid-back pubs, you'll find something to suit every taste. 
Pick up a ticket that gets you access to top bars and clubs, with some drinks secured in advance. You're guaranteed a unique and unforgettable night out in the Dutch capital.

experiences.name: Amsterdam Walking Tours
experiences.description: Discover the real beauty of Amsterdam on foot. Walk past the Anne Frank House, admire the exterior of the Rijksmuseum, walk along the Prinsengracht and Herengracht canals, and see for yourself the atmosphere of the Red Light District.

experiences.name: Houseboat Museum Amsterdam
experiences.description: Explore the interior of a real houseboat and understand the lifestyle of its inhabitants. View exhibits detailing the history and cultural significance of houseboats in Amsterdam.

experiences.name: Dam Square
experiences.description:
Dam Square is the historical heart of Amsterdam. It is also the place of lots of local and national events. At the Dam Square you can find the Royal Palace, The New Church and The National Monument.
Dam Square is located in the city centre of Amsterdam, near Amsterdam Central Station. The National Monument at the square was built in 1956 as a memoriam of the Second World War victims. Because incredibly beautiful buildings surround the square it is very popular for tourists.

experiences.name: Gardens by the Bay
experiences.description:
Gardens by the Bay is a horticultural nature park beside the Marina Reservoir in central Singapore. The park consists of three waterfront gardens spanning 101 hectares of reclaimed land. 
The gardens were designed around the idea of transforming Singapore from a "Garden City" into a "City in a Garden". They serve to increase the quality of life in the city by blending urban life with the calm of nature. The gardens are a national icon and draw millions of visitors each year.

experiences.name: Universal Studios Singapore™
experiences.description:
Singapore's Universal Studios™ unites some of the biggest stars in cinematic history for an experience that's every bit as thrilling as you'd expect. 
Prepare for white-knuckle rides, jaw-dropping live performances, and intricately detailed fantasy worlds in Singapore!

experiences.name: Minecraft Experience Singapore
experiences.description: Build structures and explore unique terrains. Interact with other players and complete challenges.

experiences.name: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark
experiences.description:
The Marina Bay Sands SkyPark is an essential experience in the Lion City, offering magnificent scenic views over the Singapore skyline. With sun-kissed city views by day, nightly light shows, and fireworks displays in the evening, sightseeing is taken to whole new heights from the incredible Marina Bay Sands Observation Deck.
So, if you're looking to elevate your Singapore experience, book Marina Bay Sands SkyPark tickets and enjoy the scenery from 200 meters (656 feet) above street level.

experiences.name: Floral Fantasy
experiences.description:
Floral Fantasy is a wonderland packed with scenes of floral artistry that smell as delightful as they look! 
Wander this magical flower-powered exhibition and breathe in the live plants, cut flowers, fragrant foliage, and preserved flowers as they come together to create a vibrant, whimsical, and immersive landscape. 
Floral Fantasy is located at Gardens by the Bay, Marina Gardens Drive, Singapore.

experiences.name: Singapore Flyer
experiences.description: The Singapore Flyer is a humongous Ferris wheel in Singapore. Towering at a height of 165 metres (541 feet) above Marina Bay, it's the second-tallest Ferris wheel in the world.

experiences.name: Singapore Oceanarium
experiences.description: Observe diverse marine life in large tanks. Attend educational programs about ocean conservation.

experiences.name: AltitudeX
experiences.description: AltitudeX Singapore is home to the world's first and largest designated wind tunnel for indoor skydiving. It's the only wind tunnel in the world of its kind, with huge windows overlooking the South China Sea.

experiences.name: Singapore City Cards
experiences.description:
Discover Singapore with a city card! Get an all-access pass to some of the city’s most iconic attractions and activities. 
With a city card, you get to experience the best of what Singapore has to offer. Explore the city’s stunning skyline, wander through its vibrant streets, and uncover its hidden gems. Buy a city card and start your journey today!

experiences.name: Singapore Night Safari
experiences.description: Singapore Night Safari is a ride through Wildlife Reserves Singapore offering visitors the chance to see nocturnal animals in their natural habitat. It is located on the Mandai Lake Road. The nearest attraction is Singapore Zoo.

experiences.name: Skyline Luge Sentosa
experiences.description: The Skyline Luge Sentosa is a unique wheeled gravity ride that gives you full control over your descent on a purpose-built track. It's located on Sentosa, Singapore.

experiences.name: Singapore Zoo
experiences.description:
The Singapore Zoo is a 28-hectare area containing over 300 different animal species. Its open and spacious enclosures are designed to imitate the animals' natural environments. Originally opened in 1973, the zoo has been actively involved in wildlife and rainforest conservation efforts since its inception.
Animal lovers can see iconic African creatures like majestic lions, chunky warthogs, and suspicious meerkats, as well as a diverse range of wildlife from all corners of the world in 11 zones representing different global habitats.

experiences.name: Singapore National Orchid Garden
experiences.description: Singapore National Orchid Garden, located in Singapore Botanic Gardens, is home to 1,000 species of orchid! On top of that incredible variety, there's also 2,000 hybrids on show, so as you walk through the garden's meandering paths you'll be constantly treated to new fragrances and sights.

experiences.name: LEGOLAND® Malaysia
experiences.description: LEGOLAND® Malaysia is a LEGO-themed amusement park located in Iskandar Puteri, Johor, Malaysia. It's the first LEGOLAND® park in Asia and the 6th in the world. The park features rides, VR attractions, themed mini cities and more.

experiences.name: Singapore Cable Car
experiences.description: Ride above the city and get a bird's eye view. Get 360° views of Mount Faber and Sentosa, across two different lines.

experiences.name: National Gallery Singapore
experiences.description: With over 64,000 m2 of cutting-edge exhibition space, Singapore's National Gallery houses the largest collection of Southeast Asian art in the world.

experiences.name: Snow City Singapore
experiences.description:
The crunch of snow underfoot, all the snowballs you could dream of making, and even a three-storey-high, 60-metre-long snow slope – it's all inside Snow City Singapore.
Along with a host of colourful mascots, simulated snowfall, and ice fishing, Snow City also has facilities for rock climbing, bouldering, and even bumper cars. If you're feeling hot, you know where to go!

experiences.name: Singapore River Cruises
experiences.description:
With its history as an ancient port and its notable modern skyline, a trip on Singapore’s waterways is not to be missed. 
Enjoy a jaunt down the Singapore River, cruise out to the bay, or watch golden rays light up the city’s most famous buildings at sunset. 
Tailor the trip to your tastes and find Singapore boat tours that come with a drink or take you to nearby islands.

experiences.name: Singapore River Wonders
experiences.description: This river-themed zoo and aquarium has a wide array of animals – aquatic and land-dwelling. It offers animal encounters, river cruises, and a general feeling of being deep in nature!

experiences.name: SuperPark Singapore
experiences.description: Designed with Finnish heart and Finnish know-how, SuperPark is a unique, purpose-built, indoor activity park in Singapore. People of all ages and sizes can enjoy the three huge areas packed with a whole list of sports, games, and activities.

experiences.name: ArtScience Museum
experiences.description: ArtScience Museum is located in Singapore. This interactive educational space in the Marina Bay Sands resort is easy to reach on public transport from anywhere in Singapore.

experiences.name: Asian Civilisations Museum
experiences.description: The Asian Civilisations Museum focuses on Singapore's ancestry and culture over the last 200 years. It is an institution forming one part of the four museums in Singapore. The other three are Peranakan Museum at Old Tao Nan School, the National Museum of Singapore, and the Singapore Art Museum.

experiences.name: Adventure Cove Waterpark™
experiences.description: Go wetter and wilder than ever before in Singapore's leading water park. Feel the thrill on pulse-pounding rides, chill out on relaxing tube-cruises and then get face-to-face with some of the ocean's most exotic creatures!

experiences.name: Wings of Time
experiences.description: Voted the number one show in Sentosa by TripAdvisor, Wings of Time is a multi-sensory spectacular – and a must-visit attraction for the whole family. It's located on Sentosa Island, in Singapore.

experiences.name: Museum of Ice Cream Singapore
experiences.description:
There is no museum in the world where you can learn about ice cream by basking in a pink Californian beach, reminiscing your childhood in the locally-inspired dragon playground or taking a dip in a pool filled with millions of sprinkles. This is the only place in the world where anything is possible. Get ready to be transported into a world where all your wildest ice cream dreams come true.
World Famous Attraction, now in Singapore!

experiences.name: Wild Wild Wet
experiences.description: Dip into something deliciously fun as Wild Wild Wet heats up the season with a CNY mala twist. A little spice, a little splash and a whole lot of CNY cheer awaits! Jump into a fiery-toned ball pit by Tsunami pool, enjoy the spirited atmosphere in A Floating Hotpot Feast, and pose among life-sized ingredients that set the scene for striking, one-of-a-kind photo ops to mark the the season.

experiences.name: SkyHelix Sentosa
experiences.description: Feel the wind in your hair on Singapore's highest open-air ride at 79 metres above sea level. Spiral your way to the top of the vertical helix-like structure and enjoy unbeatable views of Sentosa and the Southern Islands. Dangle your feet, enjoy the ride, and appreciate Singapore's stunning scenery with 360º panoramas.

experiences.name: Omni Theatre
experiences.description: The Omni-Theatre is Southeast Asia’s first 8K 3D digital dome theatre. Coupled with Southeast Asia’s largest seamless dome screen (23m in diameter), it is an immersive edutainment destination like no other.

experiences.name: Singapore SIM Cards
experiences.description:
Welcome to Singapore! Get ready to explore all the amazing sights, sounds, and flavors this incredible city has to offer with a pre-paid sim card. 
Enjoy fast, reliable internet on the go, so you can stay in the loop and connected to your loved ones. With no lock-in contracts or hidden charges, you can explore Singapore without worrying about your data usage. Get your sim card now and start planning your adventures!

experiences.name: Singapore Hop-on Hop-off Tours
experiences.description:
Explore Singapore in a fun and convenient way with the hop-on hop-off bus tour! Discover the city's vibrant sights and attractions at your own pace. 
With regular stops at all the top spots, you'll be able to hop off, explore, and hop back on again whenever you want. Enjoy the views from the open-top double-decker buses and create your own unique sightseeing experience. 
Get ready for a unique and memorable experience with this hop-on hop-off tour.

experiences.name: Bird Paradise Singapore
experiences.description: Discover the captivating Bird Paradise Singapore, a renowned avian sanctuary nestled within the exquisite Jurong Bird Park in Singapore. This enchanting sanctuary entices globetrotters from every corner of the world, luring them with the mesmerizing allure of diverse bird species. Spanning across an expansive 50 acres of verdant splendor, this sanctuary beckons with over 5,000 birds encompassing a staggering 400 species. Immerse yourself in a carefully curated tapestry of themed zones that artfully recreate the natural habitats of these winged marvels. Prepare to embark on an awe-inspiring journey through the African Waterfall Aviary, the Southeast Asian Birds Aviary, and the captivating realm of the Penguin.

experiences.name: Madame Tussauds Singapore
experiences.description: Located on the popular island resort of Sentosa, Madame Tussauds Singapore is the seventh Asian branch of the world-renowned wax museum. It features incredibly lifelike wax figures of historical characters, pop-culture heroes (and villains), and interactive exhibits that will keep everyone entertained.

experiences.name: National Museum of Singapore
experiences.description: Fittingly, the oldest museum in Singapore is dedicated to the Lion City's history. With an amazing array of cutting-edge galleries and exhibitions covering a wide spectrum of topics related to Singapore's rich heritage, the National Museum of Singapore is awash with cultural delights displayed in immaculate halls and elegant exhibition spaces that you can sink many hours of an afternoon into exploring.

experiences.name: KidsSTOP™
experiences.description: KidsSTOP™ is a kid-focused edutainment center in Singapore. It offers hands-on activities and play stations within themed zones to engage children aged 8 and under. Comprising over 3,000 square meters, it is located within the Omni Theatre building in Singapore.

experiences.name: Science Centre Singapore
experiences.description: Science Centre Singapore is located in Singapore. Dedicated to the promotion of scientific and technological education, it has over 850 interactive exhibits spread over eight exhibition galleries. The museum attracts over a million visitors a year.

experiences.name: Singapore Bus Tours
experiences.description:
Discover Singapore by bus on an exciting and informative tour! Explore the vibrant city and its stunning surroundings. Take in the sights, sounds, and smells of this amazing destination. 
Enjoy the comfort of a luxury coach as you travel around, with a knowledgeable local guide taking you to all the best spots. With regular stops and plenty of free time, you can travel at your own pace and enjoy the attractions that best suit your interests.

experiences.name: Resorts World Sentosa
experiences.description: Resorts World Sentosa (RWS is Asia’s premium lifestyle destination resort, located on Sentosa, Singapore’s resort island. It spans 49 hectares, and is home to numerous top attractions including Universal Studios Singapore, S.E.A. Aquarium, Dolphin Island and Adventure Cove Waterpark.

experiences.name: MINT Museum of Toys
experiences.description: MINT Museum of Toys houses a world-class collection of vintage toys and collectable items, including vintage posters, confectionery tin boxes, advertising signs and more in an international, award-winning contemporary building on 26 Seah Street.

experiences.name: AJ Hackett Sentosa
experiences.description: AJ Hackett Sentosa is the outfit behind Singapore's first and only Bungee Jump experience on Sentosa Island. It's easy to reach by car or taxi (follow signs to Siloso Point) or by taking the MRT train and free tram towards Siloso Beach.

experiences.name: Sentosa 4D AdventureLand
experiences.description: Sentosa 4D AdventureLand is a unique theme park on Sentosa in Singapore, which features several 4D experiences. It's located close to Imbiah Lookout and can be reached by car or MRT.

experiences.name: Clarke Quay River Cruise
experiences.description:
Clarke Quay River Cruises let visitors soak up the bustling waterfront ambiance of the Singapore River in style. Step aboard one of these luxurious sightseeing vessels and enjoy an all-new floating perspective of the busy restaurants, wine bars, entertainment spots, and retail shops all huddled together along the riverbank.
A delightful blend of modern and traditional, the bustling market atmosphere of bygone times is alive and well amidst the rows of charming shophouses and merchants. This makes for the perfect setting to enjoy a relaxing cruise.

experiences.name: Trick Eye Museum Singapore @Southside
experiences.description: Located on the popular tourist island of Sentosa, Trick Eye Museum Singapore is an optical art museum that blurs the boundaries between spectacle and spectator. Incorporating elements of augmented reality into installations and exhibits, Trick Eye Museum welcomes visitors into a 4-dimensional artistic space.

experiences.name: Mega Adventure Park
experiences.description:
Mega Adventure Park is located on the scenic Sentosa Island, Singapore's activity island resort. 
It's is a hub of adrenaline-pumping sports and attractions. With a 450m zipline, treetop rope course, a free-fall simulator, and a rock-climbing wall, it is a thrill-seeker's paradise that all the family can enjoy.

experiences.name: AIRZONE
experiences.description: AIRZONE is an elevated playground located in the City Square Mall in Singapore. Kids and adults alike can hang out in an elevated ball pit, play on giant slides and climb through nets, all across three levels of mall.

experiences.name: The Shoppes
experiences.description: The Shoppes is an enormous shopping mall located at The Marina Bay Sands resort in Singapore. The shopping center features 800,000 square feet of luxury retail outlets, cafes, restaurants, and leisure attractions.

experiences.name: Indian Heritage Centre
experiences.description: The Indian Heritage Centre is a museum and cultural centre in Singapore dedicated to the culture, heritage and history of Singaporeans of Indian descent, as well as the cross-pollinating cultural influences between South and South-East Asia.

experiences.name: Peranakan Museum
experiences.description: Peranakan Museum is a museum on Armenian Street in Singapore that specializes in documenting and celebrating Peranakan culture - the rich tapestry of cultural traditions that evolved when Chinese immigrants settled at the Malay archipelago.

experiences.name: Sun Yat Sen Nanyang Memorial Hall
experiences.description:
Sun Yat Sen Nanyang Memorial Hall is a colonial villa-turned-museum in Singapore. It commemorates Sun Yat Sen, the founding father and first provisional president of the Republic of China, who visited Singapore nine times between 1900 and 1911 during the Chinese Revolution. 
The museum features regular exhibitions dedicated to the 1911 Revolution, and the sweeping societal changes in China and South-East Asia in the following decades.

experiences.name: HeadRock VR
experiences.description: Featuring 11 state-of-the-art virtual reality games and experiences, HeadRock VR is an immersive entertainment complex on Singapore's Sentosa Island.

experiences.name: Jewel Changi Airport
experiences.description: Jewel Changi Airport is a nature-themed entertainment and retail complex inside Singapore Changi Airport with a 40-metre tall indoor waterfall, flower gardens, and interactive play areas. Travellers can also enjoy duty-free shopping and restaurants along with the views. The main entrance is found in Terminal One arrival hall.

experiences.name: Changi Experience Studio
experiences.description: Located inside the Jewel shopping complex in Singapore Changi Airport, Changi Experience Studio is a multimedia attraction where visitors can play with interactive games and displays. Its activities offer a fascinating glimpse into Changi Airport's history, and the day-to-day operations of one of the world's busiest and most award-winning airports.

experiences.name: Adventures by Asian Detours
experiences.description: Adventures by Asian Detours is a tour company based in Singapore. Their office and meeting point can be found on Pulau Ubin, which is just a short ferry ride from the mainland's Changi Ferry Terminal.

experiences.name: Flower Dome
experiences.description:
The largest glass greenhouse in the world (and easily the most spectacular), the Flower Dome at Singapore's Gardens by the Bay is home to thousands of exotic plants from five continents, arranged across a patchwork of nine breathtaking themed gardens.
Visitors can get that desert feeling while wandering gardens of spiky cacti and beautiful succulents, or stroll through a huge olive grove and see an olive tree that is thousands of years old. There are exhibition-like arrangements that mimic Dutch tulip fields, large-scale flower sculptures of famous monuments like the Eiffel Tower, and Mediterranean, South American, South African, Australian and Californian gardens.

experiences.name: Cloud Forest
experiences.description:
Cloud Forest is one of the crowning glories at Singapore's enchanting Gardens by the Bay. This giant indoor forest mimics the biosphere of tropical highlands and is filled with vibrant orchids, pitcher plants, ferns, and other rainforest vegetation. 
A winding Treetop Walk encircles a towering vertical garden where visitors meander through a blanket of ethereal mist, as the spray of tumbling waterfalls helps to keep things cool. Among all the greenery you'll find hidden caverns and secret gardens to explore, and lots of artistic sculptures poking out from the vegetation.

experiences.name: Plaza Premium Lounge Singapore
experiences.description:
The Plaza Premium Lounge is one of the most luxurious, convenient, and comfortable parts of Changi Airport – and that's really saying something! Part of an award-winning network of airport lounges, the Plaza Premium Lounge is a great place to recharge your batteries – literally and figuratively – before, during, or after your flight.
Enjoy sumptuous selections of food, including local classics like laksa or Singapore chicken rice, and a range of complimentary beverages. You can even personally talk to the chef and make a request! Take it easy in the private resting suites, use shower rooms with full amenities, drain the power outlets to your heart's content, and kick back with your favourite TV show or newspaper.

experiences.name: Red Dot Design Museum Singapore
experiences.description:
Red Dot Design Museum Singapore is a hub for the innovative and revolutionary. The building, positioned by the water in the popular Marina Bay, is itself an architectural wonder. 
Inside is a permanent collection full of ideas, prototypes, and concepts from around the world, all of which have been the recipients of the prestigious Red Dot Design Awards. Explore the temporary exhibitions and see everything from the latest in mobile technology to cutting-edge sports suits and environmentally conscious vehicles.

experiences.name: Royal Albatross Luxury Tall Ship
experiences.description:
The Royal Albatross Luxury Tall Ship – a four-masted 22-sail super-yacht – is Singapore's only luxury tall ship.
Get ready to sail out and dine on the water, while taking in unbeatable views of Singapore.

experiences.name: Ola Beach Club
experiences.description: What do you get when you combine a watersports centre where you can do paddleboarding and kayaking, a Hawaiian-themed beach club complete with Tiki-style cocktails, family-friendly facilities, and a menu of Hawaiian favourites? The laidback Ola Beach Club, where every day feels like a beach holiday.

experiences.name: Buddha Tooth Relic Temple and Museum
experiences.description: Enjoy a hassle-free visit to one of the most dynamic attractions of Singapore with a fascinating self-guided audio tour on your phone and follow, at your own pace, the riveting journey of Buddha Tooth Relic Temple.

experiences.name: Scentopia
experiences.description: Scentopia is a sweet-smelling tourist attraction at Siloso Beach, Sentosa. It focuses on Singapore’s unique floral heritage to help you create your own signature scent, based on your personality. The team's dedication to innovation led them to create a fully digitised and touchless perfume-making bar. A truly unique experience in Singapore.

experiences.name: Singapore Bike Tours
experiences.description:
Discover the beautiful sights of Singapore on a bike tour! Enjoy the city's vibrant culture and stunning views as you cycle around with a knowledgeable local guide. 
Experience the beauty of Singapore at your own pace, and learn more about the country's history and culture. Bike tours are the perfect way to get a unique perspective of the city, and make unforgettable memories. Book your bike tour now and explore Singapore like never before!

experiences.name: Singapore Vespa Tours
experiences.description:
Discover Singapore in a unique and exciting way with a Vespa tour! 
Feel the wind in your hair as you explore the city on a Vespa, taking in the sights and sounds around you. 
This thrilling tour is the perfect way to explore Singapore like a local, from its traditional neighborhoods to its iconic landmarks. 
With an experienced guide to show you around, you will have an unforgettable experience. Hop on a Vespa and discover Singapore like never before!

experiences.name: Singapore Walking Tours
experiences.description:
Explore Singapore in a unique way with a walking tour! Experience the vibrant culture of this incredible city and discover the hidden gems with an expert guide.
Get a chance to learn about the city's history and culture from an experienced local guide, and see the sights in an up-close and personal way. 
Take a guided tour to explore the iconic landmarks, stunning architecture, and colorful street murals that make Singapore so special.

experiences.name: Slingshot Singapore
experiences.description: Ride the high-speed slingshot and feel the rush of acceleration. Capture the city skyline from a unique vantage point.

experiences.name: Singapore Wildlife Experiences
experiences.description:
Looking for a wild time while you're in Singapore? Look no further.
Make the most of Singapore's tropical climate and get up close with unique wildlife, from tapirs and tigers, to lions and lemurs. Singapore Zoo promises to be one of the most incredible wildlife centres you'll ever visit, and that's even before you take the Night Safari and River Safari into consideration.
Check out the best Mandai Wildlife Reserve tickets, including Bird Paradise (the successor of Jurong Bird Park). There's cacophony of colour that's bursting from the birds inside the aviary.
Having trouble deciding what ticket to buy? Consider a Park Hopper ticket that will allow you to experience multiple parks.

experiences.name: UltraGolf Singapore
experiences.description: UltraGolf Singapore is a modern indoor golfing facility with cutting-edge simulators and immersive technology. There, golf enthusiasts can enjoy playing on world-renowned courses without leaving the urban landscape. Whether you're a seasoned pro or new to the game, UltraGolf offers a unique and exhilarating golfing experience for players of all skill levels.

experiences.name: Sentosa Island
experiences.description: Enjoy the beaches and relax by the sea. Visit Universal Studios and experience thrilling rides.

experiences.name: Marina Bay
experiences.description: Walk along the waterfront and enjoy the city skyline. Visit the observation deck for a panoramic view of the surroundings.

experiences.name: Sunway Lagoon
experiences.description: Sunway Lagoon is a multi-attraction theme park in Bandar Sunway, Selangor. Consisting of no fewer than six separate theme parks, it features a full rollercoaster amusement park, a splashy waterpark, a wildlife park, a horror-themed park, and an extreme park; it also has a Nickelodeon-themed amusement park for kids.

experiences.name: Aquaria KLCC
experiences.description: Aquaria KLCC is located on the ground level of the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre. It's currently one of the largest aquariums in Southeast Asia. Visitors can see over 150 species of marine life, including tiger sharks, sea snakes, blue rays, coral fish, seahorses and more.

experiences.name: KL Tower
experiences.description: KL Tower is also known as Menara Kuala Lumpur. It's a thrilling way to see the city from above while being right in the centre of it all. KL Tower is the tallest Telecommunication Tower in South East Asia and the seventh tallest tower in the world. The foot of the tower has areas packed with fun family activities like a zoo, an aquarium, and a rainforest canopy walk.

experiences.name: Petronas Twin Towers
experiences.description:
The Petronas Twin Towers is a sky-piercing cultural landmark in Kuala Lumpur, which was, until 2004, the world's tallest twin structures. Its architecture is Islamic-inspired and the buildings are home to the corporate headquarters of the Petroliam Nasional Berhad, among other offices. 
Visitors can experience some of the best views in Kuala Lumpur by walking on the Skybridge and visiting the viewing point on the 86th floor.

experiences.name: Batu Caves
experiences.description: Batu Caves is a popular tourist attraction not far from Kuala Lumpur. It's the site of a Hindu temple and shrine, and a limestone hill comprising three major caves and a number of smaller ones – guarded by a gargantuan statue of Hindu deity Lord Murugan.

experiences.name: G2G Animal Garden
experiences.description: G2G Animal Garden lets you get close to over 70 species of both local and exotic animals. Pet a variety of cats; touch, care, pet and play with the creatures in the animal therapy house, step into the rabbit garden that's packed with bouncing bunnies, or say hi to a slithering snake in the reptile house. Your G2G Animal Garden tickets get you hours of snuggle time. G2G Animal Garden is located in Serdang, Selangor, Malaysia.

experiences.name: Kuala Lumpur Bus Tours
experiences.description:
Explore Kuala Lumpur on an unforgettable bus tour! See the city from a whole new perspective as you take a journey through the city's many vibrant neighborhoods. Whether you're looking for a fun and educational experience for the whole family or a romantic outing for two, this tour has something for everyone. 
Take in the sights, sounds, and smells of the city as you travel through its bustling streets, and marvel at the architectural wonders and cultural landmarks that make Kuala Lumpur so unique. Don't miss out on this amazing opportunity to experience the best of what the city has to offer!

experiences.name: Skyline Luge Kuala Lumpur
experiences.description: Located at Resorts World Genting, riders can enjoy sweeping views of the surrounding mountainside as they navigate through twists, turns, and thrilling drops on the luge track. With multiple tracks catering to different skill levels, Skyline Luge promises a thrilling outdoor activity for all visitors.

experiences.name: Zoo Negara
experiences.description: Zoo Negara was officially opened in 1963 and is now known all around the world. With over 5,130 specimens from more than 475 species of mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians and fish, there's heaps to explore in a full day of animal fun. It is located just outside of Kuala Lumpur.

experiences.name: Berjaya Times Square Theme Park
experiences.description: Berjaya Times Square Theme Park is the  perfect place for a fun filled roller coaster and rides experience within the center of KL city. Be prepared for a hair-raising roller coaster ride in the midst of a shopping spree after all, it’s bound to be less frightening than all the money you’re spending in the mall.

experiences.name: KidZania Kuala Lumpur
experiences.description: KidZania Kuala Lumpur is an interactive indoor theme park, where children can experience real-life jobs in a role-playing environment. It is located in Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia.

experiences.name: Skytropolis Indoor Theme Park
experiences.description:
Head to the Skytropolis Indoor Theme Park in First World Plaza for an exciting, all-day adventure! Embrace the thrills and frills of the park, surrounded by beautiful Genting Highlands. Start your adventure by exploring the expansive indoor theme park. Test your limits and make sure to hop on one of their many exhilarating rides. Spin around on their colorful Royal Carousel, or take a heart-stopping plunge on the Sky Towers at gravity-defying speeds. The possibilities are endless! When you’re ready to take a break, grab a snack from any of their stalls scattered around the park, or relax in one of their outdoor restaurants.
Spanning over 400,000 sq ft, Skytropolis Indoor Theme Park promises endless fun for everyone in the family! Experience more than 20 rides in a convenient location at First World Plaza and adjacent to SkyAvenue.

experiences.name: Genting SkyWorlds Theme Park
experiences.description:
Genting SkyWorlds is Southeast Asia’s newest theme park, located at Resorts World Genting, Genting Highlands, Malaysia. As part of Resorts World Genting’s integrated entertainment line-up, the sky-high outdoor theme park is built across 26 acres of land and features 26 rides and attractions. 
From rollercoasters to children's rides and shows, there's something suitable for everyone in the family. All ages are welcome at Genting SkyWorlds.

experiences.name: Airport Transfers Kuala Lumpur
experiences.description: Discover the wonders of Kuala Lumpur with the convenience of airport transfers! Avoid the hassle of public transport, taxi queues, and the stress of navigation, and instead enjoy the journey with a reliable and comfortable transfer service. With a wide range of vehicles, you can find the ideal transport solution for your needs, allowing you to make the most of your time in Kuala Lumpur. Experience the beauty and excitement of the city without the worry of missing your flight - book your airport transfer today!

experiences.name: Kuala Gandah Elephant Sanctuary
experiences.description: Observe the elephants in their natural habitat. Participate in activities such as feeding and bathing the elephants.

experiences.name: Lost World of Tambun
experiences.description: Built around a natural hot spring and hugged by lush mountain jungle, the Lost World of Tambun is a theme park with a difference. Located a scenic 2-hour drive outside of Kuala Lumpur, in the township of Sunway City Ipoh, the Lost World of Tambun comes complete with a waterpark, an amusement park, a traditional tin-mining park, a tiger sanctuary, a petting zoo, and an outdoor adventure park.

experiences.name: Genting Highlands
experiences.description: Genting Highlands is a high-altitude entertainment resort in Malaysia, located about 1,800 metres above sea level. Built into the Titiwangsa mountain range, just a short drive from Kuala Lumpur, this sprawling resort is home to Malaysia's top theme park, a world-class casino, an entertainment arena, a massive shopping mall, and tons of hotels and restaurants. The Genting Skyway cable car connects the resort with the plateau town of Gohtong Jaya.

experiences.name: KLCC Park
experiences.description: KLCC Park is an urban park flanked by a massive shopping mall located in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. The greenery and water features in the space make for a refreshing retreat from the urban jungle surrounding the iconic Petronas Towers.

experiences.name: SIM Cards Kuala Lumpur
experiences.description:
Enjoy a seamless trip thanks to a local SIM card in Kuala Lumpur. 
Get access to local calls, texts, and the internet while you're exploring the city. You can use it to contact local services, look up directions, or share your experiences on social media.

experiences.name: Rajadamnern Muay Thai Stadium
experiences.description: Get a knock-out experience at Rajadamnern Muay Thai Stadium as you see Thailand's best boxers take the ring in Bangkok.

experiences.name: Ancient Siam
experiences.description: Ancient Siam is a large outdoor museum located one hour's drive from central Bangkok, Thailand. The museum grounds are spread over an area over 200 acres, which correspond roughly to the geographical shape of the Kingdom of Thailand itself. The park features replicas of Thailand's most famous buildings and monuments from antiquity and the present day.

experiences.name: Mahanakhon Skywalk
experiences.description: Step onto the glass floor and look down at the city below. Enjoy the view from one of the highest points in Bangkok.

experiences.name: Bangkok River Cruises
experiences.description:
Take to the water for a unique perspective on Bangkok by booking a river cruise in Thailand's capital city. 
Sail along the river for spectacular views of the city. Opt for an evening river cruise to see the Bangkok skyline illuminated at night, choose a fine dining experience onboard, or select a sightseeing cruise to see all the landmarks along the riverbanks.

experiences.name: SkyFlyers - Wings of Garudapterus
experiences.description: Ride the tallest attraction in the Asia-Pacific region, standing at 135 m, the height of a 36-story building. The ride’s concept is inspired by Garudapterus, which means "Wings of the Garuda."

experiences.name: Jurassic World - The Experience Bangkok
experiences.description: Explore dinosaur exhibits featuring life-sized animatronics. Participate in interactive activities and learn about prehistoric creatures.

experiences.name: SIM Cards Bangkok
experiences.description:
Delve into the sea of boundless connectivity with Airalo's eSIMs! Be a part of the 5 million savvy globe-trotters who've bid adieu to inflated data roaming fees and have adopted local eSIMs designed for their specific destinations.
Forget about juggling another SIM card, the ingenious eSIM ensures all those crucial calls and messages whizz back to the original number. Imagine this: touching down at your destination and - presto! - being connected instantly, courtesy of the wonder of an eSIM.

experiences.name: SEA LIFE Bangkok Ocean World
experiences.description: SEA LIFE Bangkok Ocean World boasts the biggest aquarium in South East Asia (unless you count the Pacific!). It's easy to reach by public transport from Bangkok, just take the BTS to Siam Station, or one of several buses heading in the direction.

experiences.name: Calypso Cabaret at ASIATIQUE
experiences.description: ASIATIQUE The Riverfront is a performance space and restaurant located in Bangkok, Thailand. It can be reached by taking the BTS to Saphan Taksin, then a taxi to ASIATIQUE.

experiences.name: Baiyoke Sky Hotel
experiences.description: Baiyoke Tower II is an 88-story skyscraper hotel in Bangkok, Thailand. It's one of the tallest buildings in Bangkok, and the 7th tallest hotel in the world. Its panoramic observation deck is a major tourist attraction, providing dizzying views of Bangkok's high-rise skyline.

experiences.name: Bangkok Bike Tours
experiences.description:
On a bike tour of Bangkok, you can effortlessly explore the city's neighborhoods, markets, and side alleys. 
You’ll get a close-up look at historic temples and local shopping areas that you'd probably miss by sticking to the main roads.

experiences.name: Bangkok Hop-on Hop-off Tours
experiences.description:
Explore Bangkok at your own pace with the convenient hop-on hop-off tours. Stay at your favorite landmarks for as long as you like with no pressure of time. 
Visit all the iconic parts of the Thai capital from beautiful shrines to towering skyscrapers with quick and easy transport options. Leave behind your worries about securing a transfer from one location to another and upgrade your trip with the Bangkok hop-on hop-off tickets.

experiences.name: The Grand Palace
experiences.description: The Grand Palace is a must-see attraction in Bangkok. This building complex has been the official residence of the Kings of Siam since 1782.

experiences.name: The Temple of the Emerald Buddha
experiences.description: You should visit The Temple of the Emerald Buddha (Wat Phra Kaew) to see one of Thailand's most revered and iconic statues. The intricate architecture and detailed murals offer an in-depth look into Thai culture and history. It's located within the Grand Palace complex, making it easy to explore multiple significant landmarks in one trip.

experiences.name: Dream World Bangkok
experiences.description: Dream World Bangkok is a family theme park packed with rides and attractions. It's easy to reach by car, or you can catch an air-conditioned bus (number 538) from the Victory Monument.

experiences.name: Siam Amazing Park Bangkok
experiences.description: Siam Amazing Park is an amusement and water park in the Khan Na Yao District of Bangkok, Thailand. It was founded in November 1980 and remains the oldest amusement and water park complex in Southeast Asia.

experiences.name: Flow House
experiences.description:
Flowrider is an extreme sport. One influenced by sports such as skateboarding, surfing, snowboarding and wakeboarding, where players surf on artificial wave simulators at a speed of 38 km/. h. to 48 km/h.
(Please pick up more from an official webite)

experiences.name: Day Trips from Bangkok
experiences.description: Explore ancient temples and ruins of Ayutthaya Historical Park. Enjoy the natural beauty of Erawan National Park and the famous Bridge over the River Kwai. Shop at one of Thailand's famous floating markets or take a wildlife safari tour of Khao Yai National Park.

experiences.name: Lumpinee Boxing Stadium
experiences.description: Watch live Muay Thai matches with skilled fighters. Purchase tickets and enjoy the atmosphere of the venue.

experiences.name: Wat Phra Chetuphon
experiences.description: Wat Pho is one of the largest and oldest Buddhist temples in Bangkok, Thailand, and features the famous "Reclining Buddha".

experiences.name: Vana Nava Water Jungle Hua Hin
experiences.description: Vana Nava Water Jungle is Asia’s first jungle-hemed water park. Located in Thailand’s beloved resort town of Hua Hin, this ecologically aware, community water park combines state-of-the-art technology, modern facilities, and over 20 rides, slides, and attractions. It's also home to Thailand’s first professional underwater studio.

experiences.name: Bangkok Golden Dome Cabaret Show
experiences.description:
Prepare for an evening of glitz and glamour at the dazzling Golden Dome Cabaret Show. For a full hour, immerse yourself in a whirlwind of sensational performances, brought to life by men transformed into enchanting female artists.
Lean back and soak in the spectacular backdrop as the artists sparkle in their breathtaking, glitter-laden outfits. Enjoy a seamless extravaganza, as the Golden Dome whisks you away on a journey through a plethora of awe-inspiring acts, each one infused with a touch of pop culture and traditional references.
This unforgettable show, combined with the opulence of the theatre and its prime Bangkok location, makes the Golden Dome an unmissable stop on any Bangkok itinerary. It's no wonder the Golden Dome recently snagged a spot in the 2017 Top 10 People's Choice Awards Thailand, as issued by the Tourism Authority of Thailand. Come, experience the magic that everyone's raving about!

experiences.name: Bangkok Self-Guided Audio Tours
experiences.description: Discover the vibrant culture and history of Bangkok at your own pace with a self-guided audio tour. Navigate through busy markets, quiet temples, and hidden local spots while listening to captivating stories and insights.

experiences.name: EasyKart Bangkok
experiences.description: EasyKart Bangkok is located in Bangkok, RCA plaza, Thailand. Royal City Avenue or RCA is one of Bangkok's largest entertainment and clubbing areas, close to several metro stations. This fun family-oriented go-karting attraction operates 7 days a week, 365 days a year.

experiences.name: Madame Tussauds Bangkok
experiences.description:
Madame Tussauds Bangkok is the perfect place to mingle with notable people who have shaped and influenced the world, from sports stars, to scientific legends to A-list movie stars. 
Madame Tussauds Bangkok can be reached by taking the BTS to Siam Station. Several bus lines also run close by.

experiences.name: Bridge Over The River Kwai
experiences.description: In Kanchanaburi outside of Bangkok lies the famous Bridge on the River Kwai, made famous in the book and later film Bridge on the River Kwai, starring Alec Guinness.

experiences.name: Maeklong Railway Market
experiences.description: Arguably the world's most precariously positioned marketplace, Maeklong Railway Market is famous due to the active railway tracks that pass right through its center packed with stalls, sending the vendors scrambling to close up their awnings, gather their goods, and move out of the way as the train passes through! It's a sight unlike any other!

experiences.name: Siam Carving Academy
experiences.description: The Siam Carving Academy is a center for budding chefs to learn the skills that go into creating beautiful garnishes and floral fruit designs. It is located close to Sutthisan MRT station in the Huai Khwang district of Bangkok.

experiences.name: Blue Elephant Bangkok Thai Restaurant & Cooking School
experiences.description: Bangkok's Blue Elephant is the world-famous restaurant of chef Nooror Somany Steppe. Opened in 2002, the Bangkok restaurant, which includes a culinary school, has been followed up by other branches in Phuket, Paris, Brussels and Copenhagen.

experiences.name: Ayutthaya Historical Park
experiences.description: Explore the spectacular temples of Ayutthaya, the ancient capital of Thailand. Visit iconic sites including Wat Mahathat, Bang Pa In Royal Palace, Wat Chaiwatthanaram, and Wat Yai Chai Mongkol on a full-day tour from Bangkok. It's the best way to immerse yourself in the culture, and get some great photos, too!

experiences.name: Wat Mangkon Kamalawat
experiences.description: Wat Mangkon Kamalawat, previously known as Wat Leng Noei Yi, is the largest and most important Chinese Buddhist temple in Bangkok, Thailand.

experiences.name: Wat Arun
experiences.description:
Wat Arun is the missile-shaped temple that rises from the Chao Phraya River's banks. Known as Temple of Dawn, it was named after the Indian god of dawn, Arun.
